### [p067_c0013] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p067_c0014] The proposed B1 would maintain its existing routing.
[p067_c0015] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p067_c0016] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.

- [p067_c0018] • Improved stop spacing

### [p068_c0003] Local
[p068_c0004] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p068_c0005] B1
### [p068_c0006] 86th Street
[p068_c0007] Service between Bay Ridge and Manhattan Beach
[p068_c0008] Related Routes: B1
> [p068_c0009] The map illustrates the B1 bus route in Brooklyn, connecting Bay Ridge in the northwest to Manhattan Beach in the southeast. The route is primarily along 86th Street, which runs north-south through Bensonhurst and Bath Beach. It then turns east onto Ocean Parkway, passing through Brighton Beach, and finally continues east on the Ocean Parkway to reach Manhattan Beach. The map includes a legend with the following symbols: Existing Service (thick green line) Added Service (thin green line) Discontinued Service (dashed black line) Proposed Bus Network (thin grey line) Subway (blue line with circles) Accessible Stations (black circle with 'A') Future Accessible Stations (black circle with 'F') LIRR (grey square) Institutional Land Uses (orange shaded area) Other map features include a scale bar (0 to 1 mile) and a north arrow. An inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of the Brooklyn area within the state of New York.

> [p072_c0009] The map illustrates the Avenue U bus service in Brooklyn. A thick green line represents the 'Existing Service' along Avenue U, running from the Gravesend area in the southwest to the Mill Basin area in the northeast. ## Page 75

### [p075_c0001] Local
### [p075_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p075_c0003] B4
### [p075_c0004] Bay Ridge - Sheepshead Bay
[p075_c0005] Related Routes: B4, B1, B64
### [p075_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p075_c0007] Existing: 9.2 miles Proposed: 9.2 miles
### [p075_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p075_c0009] Existing: 761 feet Proposed: 1,015 feet
### [p075_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p075_c0011] Existing: 2.1 per mile Proposed: 1.5 per mile
### [p075_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p075_c0013] Bus:
[p075_c0014] B1, B3, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B8, B16, B36, B37, B44, B44 SBS, B49, B63, B64, B68, B70, B74, B82, B82 SBS
### [p075_c0015] Subway:
[p075_c0016] B Q D N R F
### [p075_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p075_c0018] The proposed B4 would be rerouted in its central section. It would replace B64 service on Stillwell Av and provide service along more of Neptune Av, including to the future accessible Neptune Av F station. This new route path would increase reliability by removing turns from the route. Existing B4 service on Shell Rd and Avenue Z would be discontinued. The B1 would continue to serve 86 St and Ocean Pkwy.
[p075_c0019] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p075_c0020] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p075_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p075_c0022] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p075_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p075_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p075_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-11:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 14 24 16 30 30 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 20 20 20 20 25 SUNDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 30 27 20 27 30
```
[p075_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p076_c0005] B4
### [p076_c0006] Bay Ridge - Sheepshead Bay
[p076_c0007] Related Routes: B4, B1, B64
> [p076_c0008] The map illustrates the proposed redesign of the B4 bus route in Brooklyn. The route is shown as a thick green line, indicating an 'Added Service'. It starts in Bay Ridge, travels south along Bay Ridge Pkwy, then turns east along Avenue Z, and finally heads south along Ocean Pkwy to Sheepshead Bay. A dashed black line indicates a 'Discontinued Service' segment between Avenue Z and Ocean Pkwy. The map also shows the 'Proposed Bus Network' with thin grey lines, 'Subway' stations with blue circles, 'Accessible Stations' with black circles, and 'Future Accessible Stations' with grey circles. 'LIRR' stations are marked with grey squares. 'Institutional Land Uses' are highlighted in orange. A green box with the text 'Discontinued segment would be served by the B1.' is placed over the discontinued segment. A scale bar at the bottom right shows distances of 0, 0.5, and 1 mile, and a north arrow is also present. An inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of the study area within the state of New York.

## Page 80

### [p080_c0001] Limited
### [p080_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p080_c0003] B5
### [p080_c0004] Gravesend - Gateway Center
[p080_c0005] Related Routes: B6 LTD, B84, B103
### [p080_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p080_c0007] Existing: N/A
[p080_c0008] Proposed: 11.3 miles
### [p080_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p080_c0010] Existing: N/A
[p080_c0011] Proposed: 1,810 feet
### [p080_c0012] TURNS PER MILE
[p080_c0013] Existing: N/A
[p080_c0014] Proposed: 1.5 per mile
### [p080_c0015] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p080_c0016] Bus:
[p080_c0017] B1, B3, B4, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B9, B11, B13, B17, B40, B41, B41 XT, B42, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B60, B64, B68, B76, B81, B82, B82 SBS, B83, B103, Q35, Q51
### [p080_c0018] Subway:
[p080_c0019] 2 5 D N F L Q
### [p080_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p080_c0021] The proposed B5 LTD would be a new route primarily serving the same route path as the B6 LTD from Gravesend to East New York, with a new connection to Gateway Center. This would provide service to Gateway Center from the west, including from Canarsie and The Junction. Riders boarding west of Cozine Av/Jerome St can take either the B5 LTD or the B6 LTD. The B5 LTD would replace B84 service on Flatlands Av, and would provide service in both directions on Vandalia Av and Erskine St.
### [p080_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p080_c0023] • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
[p080_c0024] As a Limited route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than Local routes to improve speed and reliability, but still within reasonable walking distance.
[p080_c0025] Frequencies would be similar to the proposed B6 LTD, and buses would be split between the proposed B5 LTD and B6 LTD. Frequencies would still match ridership demand. Service would operate during similar hours as the proposed B6 LTD, but running later at night to provide service to evening destinations at Gateway Center.
### [p080_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p080_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p080_c0028]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 6:00 AM - 12:00 AM 10 12 10 10 15 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 6:00 AM - 12:00 AM 20 15 15 15 20 SUNDAY 9:00 AM - 9:00 PM - 15 15 20 20
```
[p080_c0029] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p081_c0005] B5
### [p081_c0006] Gravesend - Gateway Center
[p081_c0007] Related Routes: B6 LTD, B84, B103
> [p081_c0008] The map illustrates the proposed B5 bus route in the Gravesend area of Brooklyn. The route is highlighted in red, indicating it is a new service. It begins at Gravesend, follows Avenue J and Avenue H, then turns east on Glenwood Road, Flatlands Avenue, and Cozine Avenue, ending at Gateway Center. The map also shows the existing subway network (blue lines), accessible stations (black dots), future accessible stations (grey dots), and LIRR (grey squares). Institutional land uses are marked in tan. A legend in the top left corner defines the symbols for existing, added, and discontinued services, as well as the subway, accessible stations, LIRR, and institutional land uses. A scale bar at the bottom right shows distances up to 1 mile, and a north arrow points towards the top of the map. An inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of the study area within the city of New York.

## Page 84

### [p084_c0001] Local
### [p084_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p084_c0003] B6
### [p084_c0004] Gravesend - Canarsie
[p084_c0005] Related Routes: B6, B82, B103
### [p084_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p084_c0007] Existing: 8.2 miles Proposed: 7.7 miles
### [p084_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p084_c0009] Existing: 691 feet Proposed: 835 feet
### [p084_c0010] URNS PER MILE
[p084_c0011] Existing: 1.7 per mile Proposed: 1.5 per mile
### [p084_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p084_c0013] Bus:
[p084_c0014] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6 LTD, B7, B9, B11, B17, B40, B41, B41 XT, B42, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B60, B64, B68, B76, B81, B82, B82 SBS, Q35
### [p084_c0015] Subway:
[p084_c0016] 2 5 D N F L Q
### [p084_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p084_c0018] The proposed B6 would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Flatbush Av and E 80 St, the route would be rerouted via the same path as the B103 LTD. Instead of traveling on Albany Av and Glenwood Rd, the route would stay on Avenue H, improving reliability by removing turns and also increasing frequency on Avenue H. Instead of traveling on Ralph Av to Flatlands Av, the route would use Glenwood Rd and E 80 St. This would remove a challenging turn at Ralph Av/Flatlands Av, reducing delays. The proposed B6 would no longer serve Shore Pkwy and would instead use Cropsey Av in both directions. Service on Shore Pkwy would be provided by the B82 Local. The B6 would continue to run to New Lots Av overnight when the B6 LTD does not operate.
[p084_c0019] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p084_c0020] Frequencies would be slightly decreased early evenings on Saturdays and much of the day on Sundays, since there would be additional service on the corridor on the proposed B5 LTD and B6 LTD. Frequencies would still match ridership demand. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p084_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p084_c0022] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
### [p084_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p084_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p084_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 8 9 7 9 9 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 10 11 10 12 11 SUNDAY 24 hours 7 12 12 12 11
```
[p084_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 89

### [p089_c0001] Limited
### [p089_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p089_c0003] B6
### [p089_c0004] Gravesend - East New York
[p089_c0005] Related Routes: B6 LTD, B5, B82, B103
### [p089_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p089_c0007] Existing: 10.5 miles Proposed: 9.9 miles
### [p089_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p089_c0009] Existing: 1,044 feet Proposed: 1,809 feet
### [p089_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p089_c0011] Existing: 1.8 per mile Proposed: 1.6 per mile
### [p089_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p089_c0013] Bus:
[p089_c0014] B1, B3, B4, B5, B7, B9, B11, B15, B17, B20, B40, B41, B41 XT, B42, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B55, B60, B64, B68, B76, B81, B82, B82 SBS, B83, B103, Q8, Q35
### [p089_c0015] Subway:
[p089_c0016] 2 3 5 D N F L Q
### [p089_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p089_c0018] The proposed B6 LTD would mostly maintain its existing routing. Between Flatbush Av and E 80 St, the route would be rerouted via the same path as the B103 LTD. Instead of traveling on Albany Av and Glenwood Rd, the route would stay on Avenue H, improving reliability by removing turns and also increasing frequency on Avenue H. Instead of traveling on Ralph Av to Flatlands Av, the route would use Glenwood Rd and E 80 St. This would remove a challenging turn at Ralph Av/Flatlands Av, reducing delays. The proposed B6 LTD would no longer serve Shore Pkwy and would instead use Cropsey Av in both directions. Service on Shore Pkwy would be provided by the B82 Local. A new branch of the B6 LTD is proposed, the B5 LTD, mainly serving the same route path but terminating at Gateway Center on its eastern end instead of at New Lots Av. Riders boarding west of Cozine Av/Jerome St can take either the B5 LTD or the B6 LTD.
[p089_c0019] As a Limited route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than Local routes to improve speed and reliability, but still within walking distance.
[p089_c0020] Frequencies would be slightly decreased, since buses would be split between the proposed B5 LTD and B6 LTD. Frequencies would still match ridership demand. New Sunday service is proposed.
### [p089_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p089_c0022] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
### [p089_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p089_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p089_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 4:30 AM - 10:30 PM 10 12 10 10 15 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 6:30 AM - 10:30 PM 20 15 15 15 20 SUNDAY 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM - 15 15 20 -
```
[p089_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 93

### [p093_c0001] Local
### [p093_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p093_c0003] B7
### [p093_c0004] Midwood - Fresh Pond
[p093_c0005] Related Routes: B7, B13, B20
### [p093_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p093_c0007] Existing: 6.8 miles Proposed: 9.2 miles
### [p093_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p093_c0009] Existing: 662 feet Proposed: 1,167 feet
### [p093_c0010] URNS PER MILE
[p093_c0011] Existing: 2.0 per mile Proposed: 2.4 per mile
### [p093_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p093_c0013] Bus:
[p093_c0014] B5, B6, B8, B9, B10, B12, B13, B14, B15, B17, B25, B26, B31, B35, B38, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B53, B55, B60, B66, B68, B76, B82, B82 SBS, B100, B103, Q35, Q55, Q58, Q67, Q98
### [p093_c0015] Subway:
[p093_c0016] 3 4 A C B Q J M Z L
### [p093_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p093_c0018] The proposed B7 would be extended north to Fresh Pond via Cooper St/Av, 61/62 Sts, and Fresh Pond Rd for a new interborough connection. The new extension would replace a portion of the existing B13 in Glendale and would travel a path similar to a portion of the existing B20 in Bushwick. A connection to the J train would be available at Chauncey St Station instead of Halsey St Station.
[p093_c0019] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p093_c0020] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p093_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p093_c0022] • Interborough route • New connections • Improved stop spacing
### [p093_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p093_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p093_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 10 19 14 12 28 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 20 27 22 23 30 SUNDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 27 30 24 23 30
```
[p093_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

> [p094_c0006] The map illustrates the B7 bus route in Brooklyn, spanning from Midwood to Fresh Pond. The route is highlighted in green, indicating it is an 'Added Service'. The map includes a legend in the top-left corner with the following symbols: Existing Service (thick green line) Added Service (thin green line) Discontinued Service (dashed black line) Proposed Bus Network (thin grey line) Subway (blue line) Accessible Stations (grey circle with a dot) Future Accessible Stations (grey circle with a cross) LIRR (grey square) Institutional Land Uses (orange shaded area) The map shows the B7 route passing through neighborhoods such as Midwood, Flatbush, East Flatbush, Canarsie, and East New York. Key streets include Kingsway, Flatbush Avenue, and the BQE. A circular inset map in the bottom-left corner shows the route's location within the greater New York City area. A scale bar in the bottom-right corner indicates distances of 0, 0.5, and 1 mile, accompanied by a north arrow.

## Page 98

### [p098_c0001] Local
### [p098_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p098_c0003] B8
### [p098_c0004] 18th Avenue / Avenue D
[p098_c0005] Service between Fort Hamilton and Brownsville
[p098_c0006] Related Routes: B8, B17, B35, B55
### [p098_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p098_c0008] Existing: 10.1 miles Proposed: 9.7 miles
### [p098_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p098_c0010] Existing: 696 feet Proposed: 1,068 feet
### [p098_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p098_c0012] Existing: 2.0 per mile Proposed: 2.0 per mile
### [p098_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p098_c0014] Bus:
[p098_c0015] B1, B4, B7, B9, B11, B15, B16, B17, B35, B37, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B55, B60, B63, B64, B68, B70, B76, B81, S53, S79 SBS, S93
### [p098_c0016] Subway:
[p098_c0017] 2 5 3 Q D N R F
### [p098_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p098_c0019] The proposed B8 would mostly maintain its existing routing with only a minor change approaching its eastern terminal. Instead of using Remsen Av and Church Av to reach Brookdale Hospital, the route would stay on Avenue B, reducing turns and delays. The proposed B17 would continue to serve Remsen Av. The proposed B35 would continue to serve Church Av along with the proposed new B55 Crosstown.
[p098_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p098_c0021] Weekday frequencies would be slightly increased to 10 minutes or better all day, and weekend frequencies would be slightly increased as well. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p098_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p098_c0023] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor
### [p098_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p098_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p098_c0026]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 7 10 8 8 14 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 13 12 10 10 20 SUNDAY 24 hours 15 14 12 13 22
```
[p098_c0027] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 103

### [p103_c0001] Local
### [p103_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p103_c0003] B9
### [p103_c0004] 60th Street / Flatbush Avenue
[p103_c0005] Service between Bay Ridge and Kings Plaza
[p103_c0006] Related Routes: B9
### [p103_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p103_c0008] Existing: 8.0 miles Proposed: 8.0 miles
### [p103_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p103_c0010] Existing: 770 feet Proposed: 1,056 feet
### [p103_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p103_c0012] Existing: 1.3 per mile Proposed: 1.3 per mile
### [p103_c0013] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p103_c0014] The proposed B9 would maintain its existing routing.
[p103_c0015] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p103_c0016] Weekday frequencies would be slightly increased to 10 minutes or better all day, and Saturday frequencies would be increased as well. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p103_c0017] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p103_c0018] • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor
### [p103_c0019] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p103_c0020] Bus:
[p103_c0021] B3, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B11, B16, B37, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B63, B64, B68, B70, B82, B82 SBS, B100, Q35
### [p103_c0022] Subway:
[p103_c0023] D N R F Q

## Page 107

### [p107_c0001] Limited
### [p107_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p107_c0003] B10
### [p107_c0004] East New York Avenue / Empire Boulevard
[p107_c0005] Service between Broadway Junction and Prospect-Lefferts Gardens
[p107_c0006] Related Routes: B12, B43
### [p107_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p107_c0008] Existing: N/A
[p107_c0009] Proposed: 3.8 miles
### [p107_c0010] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p107_c0011] Existing: N/A
[p107_c0012] Proposed: 1,822 feet
### [p107_c0013] TURNS PER MILE
[p107_c0014] Existing: N/A
[p107_c0015] Proposed: 1.7 per mile
### [p107_c0016] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p107_c0017] Bus:
[p107_c0018] B7, B12, B14, B15, B17, B20, B25, B40, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B48, B49, B53, B60, B66, B76, B83, Q24, Q56
### [p107_c0019] Subway:
[p107_c0020] 2 3 5 B Q J L S
### [p107_c0021] LIRR:
[p107_c0022] East New York
### [p107_c0023] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p107_c0024] The proposed B10 LTD would be a new route connecting Broadway Junction and Prospect-Lefferts Gardens. Between Broadway Junction and Utica Av, it would follow the same route path as the B12 but would make only Limited stops at key destinations and transfer points. West of Utica Av, the B10 LTD would travel on Empire Blvd to its new terminal at the Prospect Park B/Q/S station, an express and accessible station. The route would also provide a faster and more direct connection to the 2/5 trains and multiple north-south bus routes.
### [p107_c0025] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p107_c0026] • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
[p107_c0027] As a Limited route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than Local routes to improve speed and reliability, but still within reasonable walking distance.
[p107_c0028] Frequencies would be similar to the proposed B12, and buses would be split between the proposed B10 LTD and B12 with an overall increase in service on the shared section between Broadway Junction and Utica Av. Service would operate on weekdays between 6:00 AM and 8:00 PM.
### [p107_c0029] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p107_c0030] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p107_c0031]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-11:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM 10 12 10 15 - Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY N/A - - - - - SUNDAY N/A - - - - -
```
[p107_c0032] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 110

### [p110_c0001] Local
### [p110_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p110_c0003] B11
### [p110_c0004] 49th/50th Streets
[p110_c0005] Service between Midwood and Sunset Park
[p110_c0006] Related Routes: B11
### [p110_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p110_c0008] Existing: 5.8 miles Proposed: 5.7 miles
### [p110_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p110_c0010] Existing: 759 feet Proposed: 979 feet
### [p110_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p110_c0012] Existing: 2.4 per mile Proposed: 2.3 per mile
### [p110_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p110_c0014] Bus:
[p110_c0015] B5, B6, B6 LTD, B8, B9, B10, B16, B37, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B49, B63, B64, B68, B70, B81, Q35
### [p110_c0016] Subway:
[p110_c0017] 2 5 D N R F Q
### [p110_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p110_c0019] The proposed B11 would mostly maintain its existing routing with only a minor Changes approaching its western terminal. Westbound, the proposed B11 would use 57 St instead of 53 St to travel between 2 Av and 1 Av, reducing travel time for customers headed to the Brooklyn Army Terminal. Eastbound, the proposed B11 would use 56 St instead of 52 St to travel between 2 Av and 4 Av. These proposed changes would streamline the route while still providing service to NYU Langone Hospital Brooklyn.
[p110_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p110_c0021] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p110_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p110_c0023] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p110_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p110_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p110_c0026]

### [p111_c0006] 49th/50th Streets
[p111_c0007] Service between Midwood and Sunset Park
[p111_c0008] Related Routes: B11
> [p111_c0009] The map displays the B11 bus route in Brooklyn, connecting Sunset Park to Midwood. The route is shown as a thick green line, indicating an 'Added Service'. It follows 49th and 50th Streets, then turns east on Avenue I and Avenue J, ending at Glenwood Rd. The map also shows the 'Proposed Bus Network' as a thin grey line, 'Subway' as blue lines, and 'Institutional Land Uses' as orange shaded areas. A legend in the top right corner defines the symbols: Existing Service (thick green), Added Service (thin green), Discontinued Service (dashed black), Proposed Bus Network (thin grey), Subway (blue line with circle), Accessible Stations (black circle with dot), Future Accessible Stations (black circle with dot and number), LIRR (grey rectangle), and Institutional Land Uses (orange rectangle). A scale bar at the bottom right shows 0, 0.5, and 1 Miles. An inset map in the bottom left shows the location of the area within the state of New York.

## Page 114

### [p114_c0001] Local
### [p114_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p114_c0003] B12
### [p114_c0004] East New York Avenue / Clarkson Avenue Service between Broadway Junction and Prospect-Lefferts Gardens Related Routes: B12, B10, B16
### [p114_c0005] ROUTE LENGTH
[p114_c0006] Existing: 4.1 miles Proposed: 4.1 miles
### [p114_c0007] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p114_c0008] Existing: 689 feet Proposed: 1,009 feet
### [p114_c0009] TURNS PER MILE
[p114_c0010] Existing: 2.4 per mile Proposed: 2.4 per mile
### [p114_c0011] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p114_c0012] Bus:
[p114_c0013] B7, B10, B14, B15, B16, B17, B20, B25, B40, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B53, B60, B66, B76, B83, Q24, Q56
### [p114_c0014] Subway:
[p114_c0015] 2 3 5 J L Q
### [p114_c0016] LIRR:
[p114_c0017] East New York
### [p114_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p114_c0019] The proposed B12 would maintain its existing routing. A new branch of the B12 is proposed, the B10 LTD. On weekdays, the B10 LTD would travel the same route path between Broadway Junction and Utica Av but would make only Limited stops. West of Utica Av, the B10 LTD would travel on Empire Blvd to its new terminal at the Prospect Park B/Q/S station. The proposed B16 would provide additional service on Clarkson Av.
[p114_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p114_c0021] Weekday frequencies would be decreased since buses would be split between the proposed B10 LTD and B12, but there would be an overall increase in service on the shared section between Broadway Junction and Utica Av. Weekend frequencies would be increased, when the B10 LTD would not operate. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p114_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p114_c0023] • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor
### [p114_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p114_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p114_c0026]

```text

```

## Page 118

### [p118_c0001] Local
### [p118_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p118_c0003] B13
### [p118_c0004] Bushwick - Gateway Center
[p118_c0005] Related Routes: B13, B5, B7, B14, B84, Q8, Q58, Q98
### [p118_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p118_c0007] Existing: 8.7 miles Proposed: 7.3 miles
### [p118_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p118_c0009] Existing: 788 feet Proposed: 1,299 feet
### [p118_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p118_c0011] Existing: 3.6 per mile Proposed: 2.3 per mile
### [p118_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p118_c0013] Bus:
[p118_c0014] B5, B7, B14, B15, B20, B26, B38, B52, B54, B55, B83, Q8, Q24, Q51, Q55, Q56, Q58, Q98, Q109
### [p118_c0015] Subway:
[p118_c0016] A C G J M Z L
### [p118_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p118_c0018] The proposed B13 would be rerouted to strengthen this critical interborough route. The route would travel in both directions on Seaview Av and Fountain Av to provide service in both directions to Shirley Chisholm State Park. The proposed new B5 LTD would travel in both directions on Vandalia Av and Erskine St. The proposed B13 would travel directly via Fountain Av, Linden Blvd, and Euclid Av to provide a direct connection between Gateway Center, residential and employment centers near Fountain Av, and the Euclid Av A/C station. The proposed B14 would be rerouted to maintain the connection between Eldert Ln and Linden Blvd and the Euclid Av A/C station. Between Jamaica Av and the Myrtle-Wyckoff Avs L/M station, the proposed B13 would travel via the Ridgewood Reservoir and Cypress Av. This would speed interborough travel, reduce turns and delays, provide new bus service to the Reservoir, while still maintaining the same subway connections and service to Wyckoff Heights Medical Center. Service along 61 St/62 St, Fresh Pond Rd, and Putnam Av/67 Av would be provided by the proposed B7. Service between Fresh Pond Rd and Wyckoff Av would be provided by the proposed Q58 Limited and Q98 Crosstown.
### [p118_c0019] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p118_c0020] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Avoids narrow streets
[p118_c0021] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p118_c0022] Frequencies would be increased on weekdays and weekends. Service would now operate 24 hours on weekdays and weekends.
### [p118_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p118_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p118_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 10 14 9 11 20 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 18 15 12 12 24 SUNDAY 24 hours 26 20 15 15 26
```
[p118_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 123

### [p123_c0001] Local
### [p123_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p123_c0003] B14
### [p123_c0004] Sutter Avenue
[p123_c0005] Service between Crown Heights and Spring Creek
[p123_c0006] Related Routes: B14, B13
### [p123_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p123_c0008] Existing: 5.2 miles Proposed: 5.6 miles
### [p123_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p123_c0010] Existing: 622 feet Proposed: 1,035 feet
### [p123_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p123_c0012] Existing: 2.4 per mile Proposed: 2.8 per mile
### [p123_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p123_c0014] Bus:
[p123_c0015] B7, B10, B12, B13, B15, B17, B20, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B55, B60, B76, B83, Q8, Q109
### [p123_c0016] Subway:
[p123_c0017] 3 4 A C L
### [p123_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p123_c0019] The proposed B14 would mostly maintain its existing routing but would be rerouted near its eastern terminal to provide a new connection from Eldert Ln and Linden Blvd to the accessible Euclid Av A/C station. Existing B14 service on Lincoln Av, Drew St, and a portion of Sutter Av would be discontinued.
[p123_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p123_c0021] AM Peak weekday frequencies would be slightly increased to match existing B13 frequencies connecting to the Euclid Av A/C station. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p123_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p123_c0023] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p123_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p123_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p123_c0026]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-11:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 12 15 11 12 21 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 18 15 12 15 23 SUNDAY 24 hours 30 20 20 20 30
```
[p123_c0027] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p124_c0005] B14 Sutter Avenue Service between Crown Heights and Spring Creek Related Routes: B14, B13
> [p124_c0006] The map illustrates the B14 bus route in Brooklyn, specifically along Sutter Avenue. The route is highlighted with green lines, indicating added service. It starts at Crown Heights (near Eastern Pkwy and Utica Av) and travels east through Dumont, Junius St, and Sutter Av, ending at Spring Creek (near Linden Blvd and Eider Ln). ## Page 127

### [p127_c0001] Local
### [p127_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p127_c0003] B15
### [p127_c0004] Bedford-Stuyvesant - East New York
[p127_c0005] Related Routes: B15, B43, B55
### [p127_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p127_c0007] Existing: 13.1 miles Proposed: 8.2 miles
### [p127_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p127_c0009] Existing: 1,009 feet Proposed: 896 feet
### [p127_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p127_c0011] Existing: 2.7 per mile Proposed: 1.9 per mile
### [p127_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p127_c0013] Bus:
[p127_c0014] B6 LTD, B7, B8, B10, B12, B13, B14, B20, B25, B26, B35, B38, B43, B45, B46, B47, B52, B53, B54, B55, B57, B60, B65, B66, B76, B83, Q8
### [p127_c0015] Subway:
[p127_c0016] 3 4 A C J M L
### [p127_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p127_c0018] The proposed B15 would be shortened on its eastern end, with all trips terminating at Drew St/Linden Blvd, and would no longer travel to JFK Airport in order to improve reliability. Service to JFK Airport would be provided by the new proposed B55 Crosstown, which would also provide additional service on Linden Blvd and New Lots Av. In Bedford-Stuyvesant/Crown Heights, the proposed B15 would stay on Ralph Av to St Marks Av and Bergen St, removing turns and reducing delays. Existing B15 service on St Johns Pl and Buffalo Av would be discontinued. Between Bergen St and Broadway, the proposed northbound B15 would travel on Kingston Av and Throop Av instead of Troy Av and Lewis Av, providing increased frequency along with the B43 between Bedford-Stuyvesant and Williamsburg and providing a new connection to the Kingston-Throop Avs C station. Existing northbound B15 service on Troy Av and Lewis Av would be discontinued. The proposed B15 would be extended on its northern end, providing a new connection to the Montrose Av L station via Flushing Av and Bushwick Av.
[p127_c0019] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p127_c0020] Frequencies would be slightly decreased, as the proposed B55 Crosstown, B43, and B65 would provide additional service along much of the route. The route would remain 10-minutes-or-better all day on weekdays though. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p127_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p127_c0022] • More direct routing • New connections • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
### [p127_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p127_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p127_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 9 9 9 10 13 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 12 10 8 10 12 SUNDAY 24 hours 14 11 10 12 14
```
[p127_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

[p128_c0006] Related Routes: B15, B43, B55
> [p128_c0007] The map illustrates the B15 bus route in Brooklyn, showing its path from Bedford-Stuyvesant to East New York. The route is depicted with various line styles: a solid green line for 'Existing Service', a dashed green line for 'Added Service', and a dashed black line for 'Discontinued Service'. A grey line represents the 'Proposed Bus Network'. The map also shows subway lines in blue, accessible stations as black circles, and future accessible stations as grey circles. Institutional land uses are highlighted in orange. A legend in the top right corner defines these symbols. A scale bar at the bottom right indicates distances up to 2 miles, and a north arrow is also present. An inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of the B15 route within the context of the entire city of New York. Discontinued segment would be served by the proposed B55.

## Page 132

### [p132_c0001] Local
### [p132_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p132_c0003] B16
### [p132_c0004] Fort Hamilton - East Flatbush
[p132_c0005] Related Routes: B16, B12, B48
### [p132_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p132_c0007] Existing: 7.8 miles Proposed: 8.7 miles
### [p132_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p132_c0009] Existing: 676 feet Proposed: 980 feet
### [p132_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p132_c0011] Existing: 1.9 per mile Proposed: 1.7 per mile
### [p132_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p132_c0013] Bus:
[p132_c0014] B1, B4, B8, B9, B11, B12, B35, B37, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B49, B55, B63, B64, B67, B68, B70, B81, S53, S79 SBS, S93
### [p132_c0015] Subway:
[p132_c0016] 2 5 B Q D N R F G
### [p132_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p132_c0018] The proposed B16 would be extended on its eastern end to provide additional service on Clarkson Av to Kings County Hospital Center and Kingsbrook Jewish Medical Center. This extension would also provide new connections to the Winthrop Av 2/5 station and very frequent bus routes such as B44 SBS and B46 SBS. The proposed B48 would serve Ocean Av. In Kensington, the proposed B16 would be rerouted via Church Av and McDonald Av, reducing a turn and thereby reducing delays, and providing a new connection to the future accessible Church Av F/G station. Existing B16 service on Dahill Rd, 12 Av, and 36/37 Sts would be discontinued. In Borough Park, the proposed B16 would be rerouted to travel via 60 St instead of narrow 56/57 Sts, providing a new accessible connection to the 62 St-New Utrecht Av D/N station. Existing B16 service on 56/57 Sts would be discontinued.
[p132_c0019] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p132_c0020] Midday and Early Evening weekday frequencies would be increased. Service span would be slightly increased.
### [p132_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p132_c0022] • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p132_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p132_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p132_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 6 15 9 15 30 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 20 20 17 20 28 SUNDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 27 20 20 20 30
```
[p132_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p133_c0005] B16 Fort Hamilton - East Flatbush Related Routes: B16, B12, B48
> [p133_c0006] The map illustrates the proposed redesign of the B16 bus route. It shows the following route segments: Existing Service: Represented by a solid green line, running from Fort Hamilton (near McDonald Av) south to East Flatbush (near Shore Rd). Added Service: Represented by a solid green line, extending from Fort Hamilton (near McDonald Av) east to Clarkson Av. Discontinued Service: Represented by a dashed black line, running from Fort Hamilton (near McDonald Av) south to 86 St. Proposed Bus Network: Represented by a solid grey line, showing the overall network structure. Subway Stations: Represented by blue circles with a white center. Accessible Stations: Represented by grey circles with a black center. Future Accessible Stations: Represented by grey circles with a black center and a dot. LIRR: Represented by a grey square. Institutional Land Uses: Represented by tan-colored areas. Key locations and streets shown include Fort Hamilton, Sunset Park, Bay Ridge, Dyker Heights, Bensonhurst, Midwood, Crown Heights, and Brownsville. ## Page 137

### [p137_c0001] Local
[p137_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p137_c0003] B17
### [p137_c0004] Remsen Avenue
[p137_c0005] Service between Canarsie and Crown Heights
[p137_c0006] Related Routes: B17, B76
### [p137_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p137_c0008] Existing: 4.6 miles Proposed: 4.6 miles
### [p137_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p137_c0010] Existing: 724 feet Proposed: 881 feet
### [p137_c0011] URNS PER MILE
[p137_c0012] Existing: 0.8 per mile Proposed: 0.8 per mile
### [p137_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p137_c0014] Bus:
[p137_c0015] B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B10, B12, B14, B35, B42, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B55, B76, B82, B82 SBS, B103
### [p137_c0016] Subway:
[p137_c0017] 3 4
### [p137_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p137_c0019] The proposed B17 would maintain the same routing as its main branch between Seaview Av/E 108 St and Crown Heights. Service on the part-time branch from E 80 St/Paerdegat 15 St and connecting directly to the Canarsie-Rockaway Pkwy L station will be discontinued. The proposed new B76 would connect E 80 St/Paerdegat 15 St and the Canarsie-Rockaway Pkwy L station in the peak hours, traveling via Avenue L and Rockaway Pkwy, and continuing via Rockaway Av to Broadway Junction.
### [p137_c0020] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p137_c0021] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Fewer route patterns • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
[p137_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p137_c0023] Weekday AM Peak frequencies would be slightly increased. At other times, overall frequencies on both existing branches combined will be maintained on the proposed routing, leading to a slight increase in frequency on Seaview Av during the peaks. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p137_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p137_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p137_c0026]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 6 9 7 5 9 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 9 8 9 9 11 SUNDAY 24 hours 9 8 9 8 16
```
[p137_c0027] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p138_c0001] Local

### [p138_c0003] B17
### [p138_c0004] Remsen Avenue
[p138_c0005] Service between Canarsie and Crown Heights
[p138_c0006] Related Routes: B17, B76
> [p138_c0007] The map illustrates the B17 bus route in Brooklyn, specifically the Remsen Avenue corridor. The route is shown as a thick green line, indicating 'Added Service'. It runs from Crown Heights in the north to Canarsie in the south. Key features include: Legend: Existing Service (thick green line), Added Service (thin green line), Discontinued Service (dashed black line), Proposed Bus Network (thin grey line), Subway (blue line), Accessible Stations (white circle with dot), Future Accessible Stations (black circle with dot), LIRR (grey rectangle), Institutional Land Uses (orange rectangle). Subway: The B17 route follows the B17 subway line, which is shown in blue. Stations: Accessible stations are marked with white circles and dots, while future accessible stations are marked with black circles and dots. Callout: A green box with a white border states: 'Discontinued segments would be served by the proposed B76.' This refers to the dashed black line segments of the route. Inset Map: A circular inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of the Brooklyn area within the state of New York. Scale: A scale bar at the bottom left indicates distances of 0, 0.5, and 1 mile. Orientation: A north arrow is located at the bottom center.
[p138_c0008] _Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign:_

## Page 141

### [p141_c0001] Rush
### [p141_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p141_c0003] B20
### [p141_c0004] Spring Creek - Broadway Junction
[p141_c0005] Related Routes: B20, B5, B6 LTD, B7, B14, B15, B55, B83, B103
### [p141_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p141_c0007] Existing: 7.4 miles Proposed: 3.8 miles
### [p141_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p141_c0009] Existing: 684 feet Proposed: 1,216 feet
### [p141_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p141_c0011] Existing: 2.7 per mile Proposed: 2.5 per mile
### [p141_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p141_c0013] Bus:
[p141_c0014] B5, B6, B10, B12, B13, B14, B15, B25, B53, B55, B60, B66, B76, B83, B103, Q24, Q56
### [p141_c0015] Subway:
[p141_c0016] 3 4 A C J Z L
### [p141_c0017] LIRR:
[p141_c0018] East New York
### [p141_c0019] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p141_c0020] The proposed B20 would be shortened on its northern end, terminating at Broadway Junction in order to improve reliability. Service between Broadway and Fresh Pond would be provided by the proposed B7, traveling a similar path via Cooper St/Av, 61 St/62 St, and Fresh Pond Rd. The proposed B20 would also be streamlined in East New York. The route would travel directly via Stanley Av between the USPS facility and Fountain Av. Service on Eldert Ln would be provided by the proposed B14. Service on Linden Blvd east of Fountain Av would be provided by the proposed B14, B15, and B55. Between Ashford St and Pennsylvania Av, the proposed B20 would travel on Stanley Av instead of Wortman Av, speeding up travel to the subway and better spacing bus routes through the area. Existing service on Wortman Av would be discontinued, though service would still also be provided on nearby Cozine Av by the proposed B5, B6, and B103.
[p141_c0021] As a new "Rush" route with a local and non-stop section, stops east of Pennsylvania Av would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing, while stops on Pennsylvania Av would only be located at key destinations and major transfer points.
[p141_c0022] Frequencies would be increased on weekdays and weekends. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p141_c0023] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p141_c0024] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Fewer route patterns • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
### [p141_c0025] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p141_c0026] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p141_c0027]

[p141_c0028] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

> [p142_c0006] The map illustrates the proposed Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign, specifically focusing on the B20 route and its relationship to other routes. The map shows a detailed street grid of Brooklyn, including major thoroughfares like Broadway, Fulton St, and various local streets. The B20 route is highlighted with a thick purple line, indicating it is a 'Routing Change'. The map also shows 'Existing Service' in a darker purple, 'Added Service' in a lighter purple, and 'Non-Stop Section' in a thin purple line. 'Discontinued Service' is shown as a dashed black line. The 'Proposed Bus Network' is shown as a solid grey line. 'Subway' is represented by blue lines with circles, 'Accessible Stations' by grey circles, and 'Future Accessible Stations' by black circles. 'LIRR' is shown as a grey square, and 'Institutional Land Uses' are shown in tan. Three callout boxes provide additional information: one for the discontinued segment served by B7, one for the discontinued segments served by B14, B15, and B55, and one for the discontinued segments served by B5, B6, and B103. A scale bar at the bottom right indicates distances of 0, 0.5, and 1 mile, accompanied by a north arrow. An inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of the study area within the state of New York.

### [p145_c0003] B25

[p145_c0005] Service between DUMBO and Broadway Junction
[p145_c0006] Related Routes: B25, B26
### [p145_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p145_c0008] Existing: 6.0 miles Proposed: 6.0 miles
### [p145_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p145_c0010] Existing: 786 feet Proposed: 938 feet

### [p145_c0014] Bus:
[p145_c0015] B7, B10, B12, B15, B20, B26, B27, B37, B38, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B48, B49, B52, B53, B54, B57, B60, B61, B62, B63, B65, B66, B67, B69, B76, B83, Q24, Q56
### [p145_c0016] Subway:
[p145_c0017] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q D N R F G J Z L S
### [p145_c0018] LIRR:
[p145_c0019] Atlantic Terminal, East New York
### [p145_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p145_c0021] The proposed B25 would maintain its existing routing all times except overnight. Overnight, the route would travel between Broadway Junction and Franklin Av. Overnight service between Franklin Av and Downtown Brooklyn would be available on the subway via the accessible Franklin Av A station or via the proposed B26, which would be rerouted via Fulton St west of Bedford/Nostrand Avs.
[p145_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p145_c0023] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p145_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p145_c0025] • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p145_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p145_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change

## Page 149

### [p149_c0001] Rush
### [p149_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p149_c0003] B26
### [p149_c0004] Fulton Street / Halsey Street
[p149_c0005] Service between Downtown Brooklyn and Ridgewood
[p149_c0006] Related Routes: B26, B25
### [p149_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p149_c0008] Existing: 6.0 miles Proposed: 5.9 miles
### [p149_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p149_c0010] Existing: 768 feet Proposed: 1,106 feet
### [p149_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p149_c0012] Existing: 1.8 per mile Proposed: 1.7 per mile
### [p149_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p149_c0014] Bus:
[p149_c0015] B7, B13, B15, B25, B27, B37, B38, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B48, B49, B52, B53, B54, B57, B61, B62, B63, B65, B66, B67, B69, Q55, Q58, Q98
### [p149_c0016] Subway:
[p149_c0017] 2 3 4 5 A C B D N R F G J M L S
### [p149_c0018] LIRR:
[p149_c0019] Atlantic Terminal
### [p149_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p149_c0021] The proposed B26 Rush would mostly maintain its existing routing, except for a streamlining approaching Fulton St. The proposed route would travel between Halsey St and Fulton St via Bedford/Nostrand Avs, reducing turns and delays. It would also provide a direct connection to the future accessible Nostrand Av A/C express station and to the accessible Franklin Av C/S station. Existing B26 service on Putnam Av would be discontinued.
### [p149_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p149_c0023] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
[p149_c0024] As a new "Rush" route with a local and non-stop section, stops along Halsey St would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing, while stops along Fulton St would only be located at key destinations and major transfer points. However, overnight when the proposed B25 would terminate at Franklin Av, the proposed B26 would make all stops between Franklin Av and Downtown Brooklyn.
[p149_c0025] Weekday PM Peak frequencies would be slightly increased. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p149_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p149_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p149_c0028]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 8 10 8 10 17 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 14 10 9 10 15 SUNDAY 24 hours 23 16 10 12 19
```
[p149_c0029] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p150_c0002] Draft Plan | 149
### [p150_c0003] Rush

### [p150_c0005] B26 Fulton Street / Halsey Street
[p150_c0006] Service between Downtown Brooklyn and Ridgewood Related Routes: B26, B25
> [p150_c0007] The map illustrates the proposed redesign of the B26 bus route in Brooklyn. The route is shown as a thick purple line, indicating a routing change. It starts in Downtown Brooklyn near Fulton Street and Tillery St, travels south along Fulton Street, then turns east along Halsey Street. A dashed black line indicates a discontinued section of the route between Putnam Av and Halsey St. The map also shows the subway network, accessible stations (black dots), and future accessible stations (grey dots). Institutional land uses are highlighted in orange. A scale bar at the bottom right shows distances of 0, 0.5, and 1 mile. A north arrow is also present.

## Page 153

### [p153_c0001] Local
### [p153_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p153_c0003] B27
### [p153_c0004] Red Hook - Vinegar Hill
[p153_c0005] Related Routes: B57, B62
### [p153_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p153_c0007] Existing: N/A
[p153_c0008] Proposed: 3.4 miles
### [p153_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p153_c0010] Existing: N/A
[p153_c0011] Proposed: 1,030 feet
### [p153_c0012] URNS PER MILE
[p153_c0013] Existing: N/A
[p153_c0014] Proposed: 2.5 per mile
### [p153_c0015] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p153_c0016] Bus:
[p153_c0017] B25, B26, B38, B41, B41 XT, B45, B48, B52, B54, B57, B61, B62, B63, B65, B67, B81
### [p153_c0018] Subway:
[p153_c0019] 2 3 4 5 A C F G R
### [p153_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p153_c0021] The proposed B27 would be a new route connecting Red Hook and Vinegar Hill/Brooklyn Navy Yard via Cobble Hill and Downtown Brooklyn. This route would replace existing B57 service between Red Hook and Vinegar Hill, with an extension to York St to replace existing B62 service between Vinegar Hill/Brooklyn Navy Yard and Downtown Brooklyn.
[p153_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p153_c0023] Service frequencies and span would resemble existing B57 service.
### [p153_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p153_c0025] • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p153_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p153_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p153_c0028]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 4:00 AM - 1:00 AM 14 18 14 14 29 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 AM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 4:30 AM - 1:00 AM 20 20 14 16 20 SUNDAY 4:30 AM - 1:00 AM 27 20 20 22 30
```
[p153_c0029] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p154_c0003] B27 Red Hook - Vinegar Hill
[p154_c0004] Related Routes: B57, B62
> [p154_c0005] The map illustrates the B27 bus route in Brooklyn, highlighting a new route (thick green line) and routing changes (thin green line). The route starts at York St and Navy St, travels south on Gold St, then west on Tillery St, and south on Jay St. It then turns west on Livingston St, passing through Borough Hall. The route continues south on Smith St, then west on Clinton St, and finally south on Lorraine St, ending at Dwight St and Olsego St. The map also shows existing service (thick grey line), discontinued service (dashed grey line), and proposed bus network (thin grey line). Subway lines are shown in blue, with accessible stations marked by a grey circle and future accessible stations by a black circle. Institutional land uses are indicated by orange shading. The map includes a legend, a scale bar (0 to 1 mile), and an inset map of New York City showing the location of the route.

## Page 159

### [p159_c0001] Local
### [p159_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p159_c0003] B35
### [p159_c0004] Church Avenue / 39th Street
[p159_c0005] Service between Brownsville and Sunset Park
[p159_c0006] Related Routes: B35, B35 LTD, B55
### [p159_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p159_c0008] Existing: 4.3 miles Proposed: 6.5 miles
### [p159_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p159_c0010] Existing: 716 feet Proposed: 923 feet
### [p159_c0011] URNS PER MILE
[p159_c0012] Existing: 1.3 per mile Proposed: 0.9 per mile
### [p159_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p159_c0014] Bus:
[p159_c0015] B7, B8, B15, B16, B17, B37, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B55, B60, B63, B67, B68, B70, B76, B81
### [p159_c0016] Subway:
[p159_c0017] 2 5 B Q D F G
### [p159_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p159_c0019] The proposed B35 would maintain the same routing as the existing overnight route, traveling between 39 St/1 Av and Mother Gaston Blvd/Hegeman Av at all times. Existing B35 LTD service would be replaced by the proposed new B55 Crosstown connecting Church Av/McDonald Av and JFK Airport via Church Av, New Lots Av, and Linden Blvd.
[p159_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p159_c0021] Frequencies would be the same as the existing B35 Local, with a slight increase in the Weekday PM Peak. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p159_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p159_c0023] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
### [p159_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p159_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p159_c0026]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 10 12 10 10 11 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 12 12 12 12 12 SUNDAY 24 hours 11 11 12 11 12
```
[p159_c0027] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

> [p160_c0009] The map illustrates the B35 bus route in Brooklyn, showing its path from Brownsville in the east to Sunset Park in the west. The route is primarily shown as a thick green line, indicating 'Added Service'. It follows Church Avenue through the center of the borough, passing through Crown Heights, Prospect Lefferts Gardens, East Flatbush, and Remsen Village. In the west, it follows 39th Street through Sunset Park. The map also shows the 'Proposed Bus Network' as a thin grey line, 'Subway' as blue lines with circles, 'Accessible Stations' as grey circles, 'Future Accessible Stations' as black circles, 'LIRR' as grey squares, and 'Institutional Land Uses' as tan shaded areas. A legend in the bottom right corner defines these symbols. A scale bar shows 0, 0.5, and 1 mile. A north arrow is also present. An inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of the B35 route within the greater New York City area.

## Page 171

### [p171_c0001] Local
### [p171_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p171_c0003] B38
### [p171_c0004] DeKalb/Lafayette Avenues
[p171_c0005] Service between Downtown Brooklyn and Ridgewood
[p171_c0006] Related Routes: B38, B38 LTD, B57, Q54
### [p171_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p171_c0008] Existing: 5.7 miles Proposed: 5.7 miles
### [p171_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p171_c0010] Existing: 696 feet Proposed: 1,043 feet
### [p171_c0011] URNS PER MILE
[p171_c0012] Existing: 1.4 per mile Proposed: 1.4 per mile
### [p171_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p171_c0014] Bus:
[p171_c0015] B7, B13, B15, B25, B26, B27, B37, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B48, B52, B53, B54, B57, B61, B62, B63, B65, B66, B67, B69, Q55, Q58, Q98
### [p171_c0016] Subway:
[p171_c0017] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q D N R F G
### [p171_c0018] LIRR:
[p171_c0019] Atlantic Terminal
### [p171_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p171_c0021] The proposed B38 would maintain the same routing as its Seneca Av branch. Existing B38 service on Stanhope St, Woodward Av, Starr St, Metropolitan Av, and Grandview Av would be discontinued. Service would still be provided by the proposed B57 on nearby Flushing Av with increased frequencies and by the proposed Q54 on Metropolitan Av with increased frequencies.
[p171_c0022] The existing B38 Local and B38 Limited would be combined into one proposed B38 Local route, with stops spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability. All B38 buses will stop at all proposed B38 stops.
[p171_c0023] Frequencies would be the same as the existing B38 Local and B38 Limited combined, leading to an increase in frequency on Seneca Av. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p171_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p171_c0025] • Interborough route • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
### [p171_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p171_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p171_c0028]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 3 4 4 5 12 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 9 7 7 7 11 SUNDAY 24 hours 11 7 7 8 14
```
[p171_c0029] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

> [p172_c0009] The map illustrates the B38 bus route in Brooklyn, connecting downtown to Ridgewood. The route is shown as a thick green line with arrows indicating the direction of travel. It starts at the intersection of Tillary St and Adams St, travels south along Adams St, then turns east along DeKalb Av. The route continues east along DeKalb Av, then turns south along Lafayette Av, and finally turns east along DeKalb Av again, ending at the intersection of DeKalb Av and Seneca Av. ### [p176_c0001] Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign:

The route is shown as a thick green line, indicating 'Existing Service'. The map includes various street names such as Delancey St, Essex St, Pitt St, Columbia St, Lewis St, Cherry St, Broadway, and Halcyon St. Parks like Seward Park, Hamilton Fish Park, and Domino Park are also marked. A legend in the top right corner defines the symbols used: Existing Service (thick green line), Added Service (thin green line), Discontinued Service (thick black line), Proposed Bus Network (thin grey line), Subway (blue line with circle), Accessible Stations (black circle), Future Accessible Stations (grey circle), LIRR (grey square), and Institutional Land Uses (orange shaded area). ## Page 178

### [p178_c0001] Rush
### [p178_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p178_c0003] B40
### [p178_c0004] Flatbush Avenue / Avenue N
[p178_c0005] Service between Prospect-Lefferts Gardens and Bergen Beach
[p178_c0006] Related Routes: B41, B41 LTD, B41 Crosstown
### [p178_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p178_c0008] Existing: N/A
[p178_c0009] Proposed: 4.9 miles
### [p178_c0010] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p178_c0011] Existing: N/A
[p178_c0012] Proposed: 2,345 feet
### [p178_c0013] URNS PER MILE
[p178_c0014] Existing: N/A
[p178_c0015] Proposed: 0.6 per mile
### [p178_c0016] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p178_c0017] Bus:
[p178_c0018] B3, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B10, B11, B12, B16, B35, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B48, B49, B55, B81, B82, B82 SBS, B103, Q35
### [p178_c0019] Subway:
[p178_c0020] 2 5 3 Q S
### [p178_c0021] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p178_c0022] The proposed B40 Rush would be a new route replacing the Avenue N/Veterans Av branch of the existing B41. The route would be shortened on its northern end to Flatbush Av/Empire Blvd to improve route reliability. The proposed B41 Local and B41 Crosstown would continue to serve Flatbush Av between Empire Blvd and Downtown Brooklyn.
[p178_c0023] As a new "Rush" route with a local and non-stop section, stops along Avenue N and Veterans Av would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing, while stops along Flatbush Av would only be located at key destinations and major transfer points.
[p178_c0024] Weekday frequencies would be decreased as compared to the existing B41 Avenue N/Veterans Av branch but would still match ridership demand. Weekend frequencies would match the existing B41 Avenue N/Veterans Av branch. The route would operate 24 hours on weekdays and weekends.
### [p178_c0025] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p178_c0026] • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p178_c0027] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p178_c0028] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p178_c0029]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 10 15 10 12 20 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 30 20 20 20 30 SUNDAY 24 hours 30 20 20 30 30
```
[p178_c0030] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 181

### [p181_c0001] Local
### [p181_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p181_c0003] B41
### [p181_c0004] Flatbush Avenue
[p181_c0005] Service between Downtown Brooklyn and Kings Plaza
[p181_c0006] Related Routes: B41, B25, B26, B38, B40, B41 Crosstown, B52
### [p181_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p181_c0008] Existing: 7.5 miles Proposed: 7.2 miles
### [p181_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p181_c0010] Existing: 985 feet Proposed: 1,097 feet
### [p181_c0011] URNS PER MILE
[p181_c0012] Existing: 0.7 per mile Proposed: 0.8 per mile
### [p181_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p181_c0014] Bus:
[p181_c0015] B3, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B10, B11, B12, B16, B25, B26, B27, B35, B37, B38, B40, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B48, B49, B52, B55, B57, B61, B62, B63, B65, B67, B69, B81, B82, B82 SBS, B100, B103, Q35
### [p181_c0016] Subway:
[p181_c0017] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q D N R F G S
[p181_c0018] LIRR: Atlantic Terminal
### [p181_c0019] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p181_c0020] The proposed B41 would maintain the same routing as its Kings Plaza branch. Service on Avenue N/Veterans Av would be provided by the new proposed B40 Rush, whose northern terminal would be at Flatbush Av/Empire Blvd. In Downtown Brooklyn, the proposed B41 would turn around via Court St and Joralemon St instead of via Cadman Plaza W, Tillary St, and Adams St in order to reduce delays, pending further study by NYC DOT. The proposed B25, B26, B38, and B52 would still provide service on Cadman Plaza W and Tillary St.
[p181_c0021] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p181_c0022] Frequencies would be the same as on existing B41 Local trips to Kings Plaza. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p181_c0023] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p181_c0024] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
### [p181_c0025] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p181_c0026] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p181_c0027]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 8 10 8 10 20 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 8 10 8 10 15 SUNDAY 24 hours 10 12 10 12 20
```
[p181_c0028] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 185

### [p185_c0001] Crosstown/SBS
### [p185_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p185_c0003] B41
### [p185_c0004] Flatbush Avenue
[p185_c0005] Service between Downtown Brooklyn and Kings Plaza
[p185_c0006] Related Routes: B25, B26, B38, B40, B41 LTD, B52
### [p185_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p185_c0008] Existing: 7.5 miles Proposed: 7.2 miles
### [p185_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p185_c0010] Existing: 1,832 feet Proposed: 2,522 feet
### [p185_c0011] URNS PER MILE
[p185_c0012] Existing: 0.7 per mile Proposed: 0.8 per mile
### [p185_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p185_c0014] Bus:
[p185_c0015] B3, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B10, B11, B12, B16, B25, B26, B27, B35, B37, B38, B40, B41, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B48, B49, B52, B55, B57, B61, B62, B63, B65, B67, B69, B81, B82, B82 SBS, B103, Q35
### [p185_c0016] Subway:
[p185_c0017] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q D N R F G S
[p185_c0018] LIRR: Atlantic Terminal
### [p185_c0019] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p185_c0020] The proposed B41 Crosstown would maintain the same routing as the existing Kings Plaza branch of the B41 LTD. Service on Avenue N/Veterans Av would be provided by the new proposed B40 Rush, whose northern terminal would be at Flatbush Av/Empire Blvd. In Downtown Brooklyn, the proposed B41 Crosstown would turn around via Court St and Joralemon St instead of via Cadman Plaza W, Tillary St, and Adams St in order to reduce delays, pending further study by NYC DOT. The proposed B25, B26, B38, and B52 would still provide service on Cadman Plaza W and Tillary St.
### [p185_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p185_c0022] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Priority Corridor
[p185_c0023] As a Crosstown/SBS route, stops would only be located at key destinations and transfer points to provide faster and more reliable service across the corridor.
[p185_c0024] Frequencies would be the same as on existing B41 LTD trips to Kings Plaza. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p185_c0025] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p185_c0026] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p185_c0027]

### [p188_c0016] Subway:
### [p188_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p188_c0018] The proposed B42 would maintain its existing routing.
[p188_c0019] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p188_c0020] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p188_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p188_c0022] • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p188_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p188_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p188_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 5 10 8 7 10 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 14 15 13 10 13 SUNDAY 24 hours 15 15 15 15 15
```
[p188_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p189_c0001] Local
[p189_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p189_c0003] B42
### [p189_c0004] Rockaway Parkway
[p189_c0005] Service between Canarsie Pier and Rockaway Parkway Station
[p189_c0006] Related Routes: B42
> [p189_c0007] The map illustrates the B42 bus route in Canarsie, Brooklyn. The route is shown as a thick green line, indicating 'Added Service'. It begins at Canarsie Pier (marked with a bus icon) and runs along Rockaway Parkway, passing through several residential and institutional areas. The map includes a legend in the upper right corner defining various symbols: Existing Service (thick green line), Added Service (thick green line), Discontinued Service (thick black line), Proposed Bus Network (thin grey line), Subway (blue line with circle), Accessible Stations (black circle), Future Accessible Stations (grey circle), LIRR (grey square), and Institutional Land Uses (orange rectangle). A scale bar at the bottom right shows distances of 0, 0.25, and 0.5 Miles. A north arrow is also present. An inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of Canarsie within the greater New York City area. The map also shows surrounding streets such as Glenwood Rd, Rockaway Pkwy, and various East 80th Street locations.
[p189_c0008] _Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign: Draft Plan | 188_

```text
Direction On Street/At Street Proposal New Stop Sequence Proposal Note Northbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/SHORE PKWY SERVICE RD N Keep 1 Northbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/SKIDMORE AV Remove Removed to improve speed & reliability Northbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/SEAVIEW AV Keep 2 Northbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/AVENUE N Keep 3 Northbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/AVENUE M Keep 4 Northbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/AVENUE L Keep 5 Northbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/AVENUE K Keep 6 Northbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/AVENUE J Keep 7 Northbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/TRUCKLEMANS LN Keep 8 Northbound ROCKAWAY PKWY STATION Keep 9 Southbound ROCKAWAY PKWY STATION Keep 1 Southbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/GLENWOOD RD Remove Removed to improve speed & reliability Southbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/FLATLANDS AV Keep 2 Southbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/AVENUE J Keep 3 Southbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/AVENUE K Keep 4 Southbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/AVENUE L Keep 5 Southbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/AVENUE M Keep 6 Southbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/AVENUE N Keep 7 Southbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/SEAVIEW AV Keep 8 Southbound ROCKAWAY PKWY/SCHENCK ST Keep 9
```

## Page 191

### [p191_c0001] Local
### [p191_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p191_c0003] B43
### [p191_c0004] Greenpoint - Prospect-Lefferts Gardens
[p191_c0005] Related Routes: B43, B15
### [p191_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p191_c0007] Existing: 6.8 miles Proposed: 6.9 miles
### [p191_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p191_c0009] Existing: 710 feet Proposed: 934 feet
### [p191_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p191_c0011] Existing: 1.8 per mile Proposed: 1.6 per mile
### [p191_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p191_c0013] Bus:
[p191_c0014] B10, B12, B15, B17, B25, B26, B38, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B47, B48, B49, B52, B53, B54, B57, B62, B65, B66, B69, Q54, Q59, Q68
### [p191_c0015] Subway:
[p191_c0016] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q G J M L S
### [p191_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p191_c0018] The proposed B43 would maintain its existing northbound routing. Southbound between Flushing Av and Empire Blvd, the proposed B43 would travel on Marcus Garvey Blvd and Albany Av instead of Tompkins Av and Brooklyn Av, providing increased frequency along with the B15 between Williamsburg and Bedford-Stuyvesant. Southbound service on Albany Av also would better fill a network gap in Crown Heights between Nostrand Av and Utica Av. Existing southbound B43 service on Tompkins Av and Brooklyn Av would be discontinued.
### [p191_c0019] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p191_c0020] • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
[p191_c0021] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p191_c0022] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p191_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p191_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p191_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 8 15 12 10 16 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 20 15 12 16 23 SUNDAY 24 hours 20 20 20 20 20
```
[p191_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.
