## Page 196

### [p196_c0001] Local
### [p196_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p196_c0003] B44
### [p196_c0004] Nostrand/Rogers Avenues
[p196_c0005] Service between Bedford-Stuyvesant and Sheepshead Bay
[p196_c0006] Related Routes: B44, B44 SBS, B49
### [p196_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p196_c0008] Existing: 8.6 miles Proposed: 8.5 miles
### [p196_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p196_c0010] Existing: 725 feet Proposed: 939 feet
### [p196_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p196_c0012] Existing: 0.5 per mile Proposed: 0.4 per mile
### [p196_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p196_c0014] Bus:
[p196_c0015] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B10, B11, B12, B16, B25, B26, B31, B35, B36, B38, B40, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44 SBS, B45, B48, B49, B52, B53, B54, B55, B57, B65, B68, B69, B81, B82, B82 SBS, B100, B103, Q35
### [p196_c0016] Subway:
[p196_c0017] 2 3 5 A C G S
### [p196_c0018] LIRR:
[p196_c0019] Nostrand Avenue
### [p196_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p196_c0021] The proposed B44 would maintain its existing southbound routing. Northbound between Flatbush Av and Fulton St, the proposed B44 would travel on Rogers Av to match the B44 SBS. This would reduce travel time and increase reliability by reducing route length and by utilizing existing bus lanes on Rogers Av. Northbound service on New York Av would be provided by the proposed B49. The B44 would continue to run to Williamsburg Bridge Plaza overnight when the B44 SBS does not operate.
### [p196_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p196_c0023] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor
[p196_c0024] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p196_c0025] Weekday frequencies would be increased. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p196_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p196_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p196_c0028]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 7 8 7 8 11 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 11 9 8 9 10 SUNDAY 24 hours 11 11 10 10 12
```
[p196_c0029] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

> [p197_c0007] The map displays the Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign. The B44 route is highlighted in green, running north-south through the center of Brooklyn. It starts at Bedford-Stuyvesant (north) and ends at Sheepshead Bay (south). The route passes through neighborhoods like Clinton Hill, Bushwick, Crown Heights, Flatbush, Midwood, and Gravesend. Key landmarks include Prospect Park, Green-Wood Cemetery, and the East River. A legend in the top right corner defines the symbols: Existing Service (thick green line), Added Service (thin green line), Discontinued Service (thick black line), Proposed Bus Network (thin grey line), Subway (blue line with circle), Accessible Stations (black circle with dot), Future Accessible Stations (grey circle with dot), LIRR (grey square), and Institutional Land Uses (orange area). A callout box points to a discontinued segment of the B44 route, stating it would be served by the proposed B49. An inset map in the bottom left shows the location of the study area within the state of New York. A scale bar and north arrow are located in the bottom right corner.
[p197_c0008] _Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign: Draft Plan | 196_

## Page 201

### [p201_c0001] Crosstown/SBS
### [p201_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p201_c0003] B44
### [p201_c0004] Nostrand/Rogers Avenues
[p201_c0005] Service between Williamsburg and Sheepshead Bay
[p201_c0006] Related Routes: B44 SBS, B36, B44
### [p201_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p201_c0008] Existing: 9.4 miles Proposed: 10.0 miles
### [p201_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p201_c0010] Existing: 2,608 feet Proposed: 2,640 feet
### [p201_c0011] URNS PER MILE
[p201_c0012] Existing: 0.6 per mile Proposed: 0.5 per mile
### [p201_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p201_c0014] Bus:
[p201_c0015] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B10, B11, B12, B16, B25, B26, B31, B35, B36, B38, B39, B40, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B45, B48, B49, B52, B53, B55 SBS, B57, B62, B66, B68, B69, B81, B82, B82 SBS, B100, B103, Q35, Q54, Q59, Q68
### [p201_c0016] Subway:
[p201_c0017] 2 5 A C G J M Z S
[p201_c0018] LIRR: Nostrand Avenue
### [p201_c0019] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p201_c0020] The proposed B44 SBS would mostly maintain its existing routing but would be rerouted near its southern terminal to Ocean Pkwy via Avenue Z. This would provide a new connection to the future accessible Sheepshead Bay B/Q station and additional service to Coney Island Hospital. The proposed B44 Local would continue to serve Shore Pkwy and Emmons Av.
[p201_c0021] As a Crosstown/SBS route, stops would only be located at key destinations and transfer points to provide faster and more reliable service across the corridor.
[p201_c0022] Frequencies would be increased Late Evening on all days, and Early Morning and Early Evening on Sundays. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p201_c0023] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p201_c0024] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p201_c0025] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p201_c0026] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p201_c0027]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 5:30 AM - 11:00 PM 5 8 6 7 10 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM 9 8 8 9 13 SUNDAY 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM 12 12 10 12 15
```
[p201_c0028] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p204_c0001] Local

### [p204_c0003] B45

[p204_c0006] Related Routes: B45, B47
### [p204_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p204_c0008] Existing: 4.6 miles Proposed: 5.1 miles
### [p204_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p204_c0010] Existing: 784 feet Proposed: 1,042 feet
### [p204_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p204_c0012] Existing: 1.8 per mile Proposed: 1.9 per mile
### [p204_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p204_c0014] Bus:
[p204_c0015] B7, B10, B12, B14, B15, B17, B25, B26, B27, B37, B38, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B47, B48, B49, B52, B54, B57, B61, B62, B63, B65, B67, B69
### [p204_c0016] Subway:
[p204_c0017] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q D N R F G S
### [p204_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p204_c0019] The proposed B45 would mostly maintain its existing routing. The route would be extended to the Ralph Av C station via St Johns Pl and Ralph Av on its eastern end, providing better transit connections to points east in Brooklyn and Queens. Service on Buffalo Av would be discontinued. Westbound, the B45 would be rerouted via Sterling Pl between New York Av and Rogers Av to avoid a narrow two-way section of St Johns Pl. In Downtown Brooklyn, the proposed B45 would turn around in the opposite direction via Livingston St, Court St, and Joralemon St in order to reduce delays, pending further study by NYC DOT.
[p204_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p204_c0021] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p204_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p204_c0023] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
### [p204_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p204_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p204_c0026]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 5:00 AM - 1:30 AM 12 15 11 14 24 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 5:00 AM - 1:30 AM 23 15 13 13 19 SUNDAY 5:00 AM - 1:30 AM 27 27 20 20 28
```
[p204_c0027] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

[p208_c0006] Related Routes: B46, B53

[p208_c0015] B3, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B10, B12, B14, B15, B17, B25, B26, B35, B38, B40, B41, B41 XT, B43, B45, B46 SBS, B47, B52, B53, B54, B55, B57, B65, B82, B82 SBS, B100, B103, Q35
### [p208_c0016] Subway:
[p208_c0017] 3 4 A C J M Z
### [p208_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p208_c0019] The proposed B46 would be shortened on its northern end, terminating at Woodhull Hospital in order to improve reliability. The connection to the accessible Flushing Av J/M station would be maintained. Service on Broadway between Woodhull Hospital and Williamsburg Bridge Plaza would be provided by the proposed new B53.
[p208_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p208_c0021] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p208_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p208_c0023] • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor

### [p212_c0001] Crosstown/SBS
### [p212_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p212_c0003] B46
### [p212_c0004] Utica Avenue
[p212_c0005] Service between Bedford-Stuyvesant and Kings Plaza
[p212_c0006] Related Routes: B46 SBS

[p212_c0010] Existing: 2,649 feet Proposed: 2,649 feet
### [p212_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p212_c0012] Existing: 0.3 per mile Proposed: 0.3 per mile
### [p212_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p212_c0014] Bus:
[p212_c0015] B3, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B10, B12, B14, B17, B25, B26, B35, B38, B40, B41, B41 XT, B43, B46 SBS, B47, B52, B54, B55, B57, B82, B82 SBS, B103, Q35

### [p212_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p212_c0019] The proposed B46 SBS would maintain its existing routing.
[p212_c0020] As a Crosstown/SBS route, stops would only be located at key destinations and transfer points to provide faster and more reliable service across the corridor.
[p212_c0021] Frequencies would be slightly increased on Weekday Middays. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p212_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p212_c0023] • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor

## Page 215

### [p215_c0001] Local
### [p215_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p215_c0003] B47
### [p215_c0004] Ralph Avenue
[p215_c0005] Service between Bedford-Stuyvesant and Kings Plaza
[p215_c0006] Related Routes: B47
### [p215_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p215_c0008] Existing: 7.6 miles Proposed: 7.1 miles
### [p215_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p215_c0010] Existing: 701 feet Proposed: 966 feet
### [p215_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p215_c0012] Existing: 1.7 per mile Proposed: 1.5 per mile
### [p215_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p215_c0014] Bus:
[p215_c0015] B3, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B10, B12, B14, B15, B17, B25, B26, B35, B38, B40, B41, B41 XT, B43, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B52, B53, B54, B55, B57, B65, B82, B82, B100, B103, Q35
### [p215_c0016] Subway:
[p215_c0017] 3 C J M Z
### [p215_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p215_c0019] The proposed B47 would mostly maintain its existing routing but would be streamlined onto Kings Hwy for a faster trip between Remsen Av and E 98 St instead of traveling via Clarkson Av and Rockaway Pkwy. This reroute would remove turns and reduce delays. This proposal is pending further study by NYC DOT of the necessary infrastructure upgrades on Kings Hwy for the reroute to be feasible.
[p215_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p215_c0021] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p215_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p215_c0023] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p215_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p215_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p215_c0026]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 7 14 10 11 18 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 17 12 11 12 16 SUNDAY 24 hours 20 18 15 17 23
```
[p215_c0027] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p216_c0001] Local
[p216_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p216_c0003] B47
### [p216_c0004] Ralph Avenue
[p216_c0005] Service between Bedford-Stuyvesant and Kings Plaza
[p216_c0006] Related Routes: B47
> [p216_c0007] The map illustrates the B47 bus route in Brooklyn, showing its path from Bedford-Stuyvesant to Kings Plaza. The route is primarily shown as a thick green line, indicating 'Added Service'. A legend in the upper left corner defines the map's symbology: a thick green line for 'Existing Service', a thinner green line for 'Added Service', a dashed black line for 'Discontinued Service', a thin grey line for 'Proposed Bus Network', a blue circle for 'Subway', a grey circle for 'Accessible Stations', a black circle for 'Future Accessible Stations', a grey square for 'LIRR', and a tan square for 'Institutional Land Uses'. The map also shows various bus stops, including 'Bedford-Stuyvesant', 'Broadway Junction', 'Kings Plaza', and 'Mill Basin'. An inset map in the bottom left corner shows the route's location within the city of New York. A scale bar in the bottom right corner indicates distances of 0, 0.5, and 1 mile, accompanied by a north arrow.

## Page 220

### [p220_c0001] Local
### [p220_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p220_c0003] B48
### [p220_c0004] Flatbush - Downtown Brooklyn
[p220_c0005] Related Routes: B48, B16, B69
### [p220_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p220_c0007] Existing: 6.0 miles Proposed: 5.2 miles
### [p220_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p220_c0009] Existing: 710 feet Proposed: 1,083 feet
### [p220_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p220_c0011] Existing: 1.8 per mile Proposed: 1.7 per mile
### [p220_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p220_c0013] Bus:
[p220_c0014] B10, B16, B25, B26, B27, B35, B38, B40, B41, B41 XT, B43, B45, B49, B52, B54, B55, B57, B62, B65, B67, B69
### [p220_c0015] Subway:
[p220_c0016] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q F G S
### [p220_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p220_c0018] The proposed B48 would be rerouted on its northern end to Downtown Brooklyn via Flushing Av to provide a new connection from central Brooklyn east of Prospect Park to the Brooklyn Navy Yard, increasing north-south service to that employment center. The proposed B48 would be extended on its southern end to Church Av via Ocean Av to provide new connections to the B35 and the proposed new B55 Crosstown to JFK Airport on Church Av, and to the B12 and the proposed B16 on Parkside Av/Woodside Av. Service on Wallabout St, Lorimer St, and Nassau Av would be provided by the proposed B69.
### [p220_c0019] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p220_c0020] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
[p220_c0021] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p220_c0022] No frequency changes are being proposed at this time. The route would no longer operate 24 hours.
### [p220_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p220_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p220_c0025]

```text

```
[p220_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 225

### [p225_c0001] Local
### [p225_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p225_c0003] B49
### [p225_c0004] Ocean/Nostrand/New York Avenues
[p225_c0005] Service between Coney Island and Bedford-Stuyvesant
[p225_c0006] Related Routes: B49, B4, B44, B44 SBS, B68
### [p225_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p225_c0008] Existing: 8.8 miles Proposed: 10.1 miles
### [p225_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p225_c0010] Existing: 721 feet Proposed: 914 feet
### [p225_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p225_c0012] Existing: 1.5 per mile Proposed: 1.1 per mile
### [p225_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p225_c0014] Bus:
[p225_c0015] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B10, B11, B12, B16, B25, B26, B31, B35, B36, B40, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B48, B55, B64, B65, B68, B74, B81, B82, B82 SBS, B100
### [p225_c0016] Subway:
[p225_c0017] 2 3 5 A C B Q D N F S
### [p225_c0018] LIRR:
[p225_c0019] Nostrand Avenue
### [p225_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p225_c0021] In its northern section, the proposed B49 would travel northbound on New York Av and southbound on Nostrand Av between Foster Av and Atlantic Av/Fulton St. Northbound service on New York Av would replace the B44 Local which is proposed to be rerouted onto Rogers Av. Southbound service on Nostrand Av would be faster and more reliable due to the existing bus lanes. Service on Bedford Av would be discontinued. On its southern end, the proposed B49 would be rerouted to Coney Island-Stillwell Av D/F/N/Q station via Avenue Z, Coney Island Av, and Brighton Beach Av, providing a new accessible connection. This would replace the B68 which is proposed to be rerouted to Kingsborough Community College. The proposed B49 would avoid Sheepshead Bay Rd and challenging turns in this area, reducing delays. The proposed B4 would continue to serve Sheepshead Bay Rd. Service on West End Av would be discontinued.
[p225_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability. All existing B49 LTD trips would operate as Local trips, making all proposed stops.
[p225_c0023] Weekend frequencies would be increased to better match existing B44 and B68 frequencies. The route would now operate 24 hours to provide overnight service to Kings County Hospital Center on New York Av.
### [p225_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p225_c0025] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Fewer route patterns • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p225_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p225_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p225_c0028]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 7 9 8 10 15 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 11 10 10 10 15 SUNDAY 24 hours 12 12 10 11 15
```
[p225_c0029] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 231

### [p231_c0001] Local
### [p231_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p231_c0003] B52
### [p231_c0004] Gates Avenue
[p231_c0005] Service between Downtown Brooklyn and Ridgewood
[p231_c0006] Related Routes: B52
### [p231_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p231_c0008] Existing: 5.1 miles Proposed: 5.1 miles
### [p231_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p231_c0010] Existing: 756 feet Proposed: 981 feet
### [p231_c0011] URNS PER MILE
[p231_c0012] Existing: 1.4 per mile Proposed: 1.2 per mile
### [p231_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p231_c0014] Bus:
[p231_c0015] B13, B15, B25, B26, B27, B37, B38, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B48, B53, B54, B57, B61, B62, B63, B65, B66, B67, B69, Q55, Q58, Q98
### [p231_c0016] Subway:
[p231_c0017] 2 3 4 5 A C B D N R F G
### [p231_c0018] LIRR:
[p231_c0019] Atlantic Terminal
### [p231_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p231_c0021] The proposed B52 would mostly maintain its existing routing, except for a streamlining approaching Fulton St. The proposed route would stay on Gates Av for longer and would use Washington Av to connect to Fulton St. This would increase reliability by removing turns and by utilizing existing bus lanes on Fulton St for a longer distance. It would also reduce travel time for customers connecting to the C train, providing a quicker connection at the Clinton-Washington Avs station rather than the Lafayette Av station. Existing B52 service on Greene Av would be discontinued.
[p231_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p231_c0023] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p231_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p231_c0025] • Interborough route • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p231_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p231_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p231_c0028]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 6 8 8 8 18 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 12 8 9 10 15 SUNDAY 24 hours 22 12 11 12 20
```
[p231_c0029] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p232_c0003] Local
[p232_c0004] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p232_c0005] B52 Gates Avenue
[p232_c0006] Service between Downtown Brooklyn and Ridgewood Related Routes: B52
> [p232_c0007] The map illustrates the B52 bus route in Brooklyn, specifically focusing on the Gates Avenue corridor. The route is shown as a thick green line, indicating 'Added Service'. It starts in Downtown Brooklyn near Adams Street and Court Street, travels east along Fulton Street, then turns south onto Gates Avenue, passing through Bedford-Stuyvesant and ending near Palmetto Street. A legend in the upper left corner defines the symbols: Existing Service (thick green line), Added Service (thick green line), Discontinued Service (dashed black line), Proposed Bus Network (thin grey line), Subway (blue line with circles), Accessible Stations (grey circle with dot), Future Accessible Stations (grey circle with dot and arrow), LIRR (grey square), and Institutional Land Uses (orange shaded area). ## Page 235

### [p235_c0001] Local
### [p235_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p235_c0003] B53
### [p235_c0004] Broadway Junction - Sunnyside
[p235_c0005] Related Routes: B24, B32, B46, Q24
### [p235_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p235_c0007] Existing: N/A
[p235_c0008] Proposed: 8.1 miles
### [p235_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p235_c0010] Existing: N/A
[p235_c0011] Proposed: 1,157 feet
### [p235_c0012] TURNS PER MILE
[p235_c0013] Existing: N/A
[p235_c0014] Proposed: 1.0 per mile
### [p235_c0015] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p235_c0016] Bus:
[p235_c0017] B7, B10, B12, B15, B20, B25, B26, B38, B39, B43, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B52, B54, B57, B60, B62, B66, B69, B76, B83, Q24, Q54, Q56, Q59, Q68
### [p235_c0018] Subway:
[p235_c0019] 7 A C G J M Z L
### [p235_c0020] LIRR:
[p235_c0021] East New York
### [p235_c0022] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p235_c0023] As part of both the Brooklyn and Queens Bus Network Redesigns, the proposed B53 would be a new interborough route serving segments of the existing B24 and B32 and replacing the two routes. The proposed B53 would serve the entire stretch of Broadway from Broadway Junction to Williamsburg, continuing through Williamsburg via Kent Av/Wythe Av, and extending across Greenpoint Av to Sunnyside, connecting to the 7 train at the future accessible 46 St-Bliss St station. The route would replace existing Q24 service from Broadway Junction to Lafayette Av (the proposed Q24 would still operate on Atlantic Av east of Broadway Junction). It would also replace the existing B32 in its entirety and the northern segment of the existing B24 on Greenpoint Av (the other half of the B24 would be served by the Queens Bus Network Redesign's proposed Q68).
[p235_c0024] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p235_c0025] Frequencies would be increased compared to existing B24 and B32. Service would operate 24 hours (between Broadway Junction and Williamsburg Plaza only).
### [p235_c0026] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p235_c0027] • Interborough route • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access
### [p235_c0028] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p235_c0029] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p235_c0030]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 14 20 15 18 25 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 20 20 20 20 25 SUNDAY 24 hours 20 20 20 20 25
```
[p235_c0031] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

It runs from the vicinity of Jay St and Flatbush Av in Downtown Brooklyn, through Clinton Hill, Myrtle Av, and Bushwick, ending at Myrtle-Wyckoff Avs in Ridgewood. The map also shows the 'Proposed Bus Network' in grey, 'Subway' lines in blue, and 'Institutional Land Uses' in tan. A legend in the bottom right corner defines the symbols: Existing Service (thick green line), Added Service (thin green line), Discontinued Service (dashed black line), Proposed Bus Network (grey line), Subway (blue line with circle), Accessible Stations (black circle with dot), Future Accessible Stations (black circle with dot and dot), LIRR (grey square), and Institutional Land Uses (tan area). ## Page 243

### [p243_c0001] Crosstown/SBS
### [p243_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p243_c0003] B55
### [p243_c0004] Kensington - JFK Airport
[p243_c0005] Related Routes: B15, B35
### [p243_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p243_c0007] Existing: N/A
[p243_c0008] Proposed: 9.4 miles
### [p243_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p243_c0010] Existing: N/A
[p243_c0011] Proposed: 2,152 feet
### [p243_c0012] URNS PER MILE
[p243_c0013] Existing: N/A
[p243_c0014] Proposed: 0.8 per mile
### [p243_c0015] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p243_c0016] Bus:
[p243_c0017] B6, B7, B8, B13, B14, B15, B16, B17, B20, B35, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B60, B67, B68, B76, B81, B83, B103, Q3, Q8, Q10, Q51, Q109
### [p243_c0018] Subway:
[p243_c0019] 2 3 5 A B C F G L
### [p243_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p243_c0021] The proposed B55 would be a new route connecting Church Av and JFK Airport. Between Church Av/McDonald Av and Hegeman Av/Rockaway Av, it would replace the B35. Between Hegeman Av/Rockaway Av and Linden Blvd/Drew St it would provide additional service to the B15 but only making stops at key destinations and transfer points.
### [p243_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p243_c0023] • Interborough route • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
[p243_c0024] The proposed B55 would replace B15 service connecting Brooklyn and JFK Airport, terminating at the Lefferts Blvd AirTrain station during JFK Redevelopment. With fewer stops than the existing B15, the proposed B55 would provide faster and more reliable access to JFK Airport. It would maintain the same connections from JFK Airport to subways and other bus routes in East New York and Brownsville, and it would provide additional service between Linden Blvd and the New Lots Av 3 and L stations. It would also provide access from new areas of Brooklyn to East New York and JFK Airport with new connections to the 2/5, B/Q, and F/G subways as well as many high-frequency bus routes.
[p243_c0025] As a new Crosstown route, stops would only be located at key destinations and transfer points to provide faster and more reliable service along the corridor.
[p243_c0026] Frequencies would be similar to or better than the existing B35 and existing B15 JFK Airport trips, and would be 10-minutes-or-better on weekdays. Service would operate 24 hours on weekdays and weekends.
### [p243_c0027] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p243_c0028] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p243_c0029]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 7 10 7 8 12 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 12 12 10 10 12 SUNDAY 24 hours 13 12 10 12 14
```
[p243_c0030] * Frequencies indicate the average headway between bus trips in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p244_c0002] Crosstown/SBS
[p244_c0003] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p244_c0004] B55 Kensington - JFK Airport Related Routes: B15, B35
> [p244_c0005] The map illustrates the B55 bus route in Brooklyn, extending from Kensington in the west to JFK Airport in the east. The route is shown in blue, with a thicker line indicating 'Added Service' and a thinner line for 'Existing Service'. A dashed line indicates 'Discontinued Service'. The map also shows the 'Proposed Bus Network' in grey, 'Subway' in blue, 'Accessible Stations' as black circles, 'Future Accessible Stations' as grey circles, 'LIRR' as grey squares, and 'Institutional Land Uses' as orange areas. Key landmarks and neighborhoods shown include Kensington, Flatbush, Midwood, Canarsie, Starrett City, Spring Creek, and East New York. Major roads like I-495, I-278, and I-277 are also depicted. A legend in the bottom right corner provides details for the route types and map symbols. A scale bar indicates distances up to 1 mile, and a north arrow is included.

## Page 246

### [p246_c0001] Limited
### [p246_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p246_c0003] B57
### [p246_c0004] Flushing Avenue
[p246_c0005] Service between Downtown Brooklyn and Jackson Heights
[p246_c0006] Related Routes: B57, B27, B62
### [p246_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p246_c0008] Existing: 8.0 miles Proposed: 8.4 miles
### [p246_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p246_c0010] Existing: 800 feet Proposed: 1,403 feet
### [p246_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p246_c0012] Existing: 1.4 per mile Proposed: 1.0 per mile
### [p246_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p246_c0014] Bus:
[p246_c0015] B15, B25, B26, B27, B38, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B46, B47, B48, B52, B53, B54, B61, B62, B63, B65, B66, B67, B69, Q18, Q21, Q32, Q33, Q47, Q49, Q52 SBS, Q53 SBS, Q54, Q58, Q59, Q60, Q67, Q70, Q98
### [p246_c0016] Subway:
[p246_c0017] 2 3 4 5 7 A C E F G L M R
### [p246_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p246_c0019] As part of both the Brooklyn and Queens Bus Network Redesigns, the proposed B57 LTD would be extended north from Maspeth to Jackson Heights via 69 St, providing new, all-day frequent service between Brooklyn and Queens, addressing customer requests for better interborough service. In Brooklyn, the proposed B57 LTD would travel via Park Av west of Classon Av and would terminate in Downtown Brooklyn. Service on Flushing Av west of Classon Av would be provided by the proposed B62 LTD. Service between Red Hook and Downtown Brooklyn would be provided by the proposed B27.
[p246_c0020] As a Limited route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than Local routes to improve speed and reliability, but still within reasonable walking distance.
[p246_c0021] Frequencies would be increased on weekdays to 10-minutes-or-better. Saturday Late Evening frequencies would be reduced to match weekdays. Service would now operate 24 hours on weekdays and weekends.
### [p246_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p246_c0023] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p246_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p246_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p246_c0026]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 10 10 10 10 25 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 18 20 19 15 25 SUNDAY 24 hours 23 20 20 23 30
```
[p246_c0027] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 251

### [p251_c0001] Local
### [p251_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p251_c0003] B60
### [p251_c0004] Rockaway Avenue
[p251_c0005] Service between Canarsie and Broadway Junction
[p251_c0006] Related Routes: B60, B5, B6 LTD, B66, B76
### [p251_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p251_c0008] Existing: 7.9 miles Proposed: 4.0 miles
### [p251_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p251_c0010] Existing: 635 feet Proposed: 976 feet
### [p251_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p251_c0012] Existing: 1.4 per mile Proposed: 1.0 per mile
### [p251_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p251_c0014] Bus:
[p251_c0015] B5, B6, B7, B8, B10, B12, B14, B15, B20, B25, B35, B42, B53, B55, B66, B76, B82, B82 SBS, B83, B103, Q24, Q56
### [p251_c0016] Subway:
[p251_c0017] 3 A C J Z L
### [p251_c0018] LIRR:
[p251_c0019] East New York
### [p251_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p251_c0021] The proposed B60 would be shortened on its northern end in order to improve reliability, traveling via Fulton St to terminate at the future accessible Broadway Junction A/C/J/Z/L station and creating new connections.
[p251_c0022] Service between Fulton St and Williamsburg Bridge Plaza would be provided by the proposed B66. The proposed B76 would provide additional service between the Canarsie-Rockaway Parkway L station and Broadway Junction weekdays during peak hours. East of the Canarsie-Rockaway Parkway L station, the proposed B60 would travel via Flatlands Av in both directions in order to simplify the route path. Service on Glenwood Av would still be provided by the proposed B5 LTD and the B6 LTD.
[p251_c0023] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p251_c0024] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p251_c0025] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p251_c0026] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p251_c0027] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p251_c0028] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p251_c0029]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 9 13 10 15 20 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 27 17 15 18 23 SUNDAY 24 hours 20 20 20 20 23
```
[p251_c0030] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 256

### [p256_c0001] Local
### [p256_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p256_c0003] B61
### [p256_c0004] Park Slope - Red Hook - Downtown Brooklyn
[p256_c0005] Related Routes: B61, B27, B81
### [p256_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p256_c0007] Existing: 5.9 miles Proposed: 5.1 miles
### [p256_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p256_c0009] Existing: 783 feet Proposed: 906 feet
### [p256_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p256_c0011] Existing: 3.0 per mile Proposed: 2.6 per mile
### [p256_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p256_c0013] Bus:
[p256_c0014] B25, B26, B27, B37, B38, B41, B41 XT, B45, B52, B54, B57, B61, B62, B63, B65, B67, B69, B81
### [p256_c0015] Subway:
[p256_c0016] 2 3 4 5 A C D N R F G
### [p256_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p256_c0018] The proposed B61 would mostly maintain its existing routing. On its southern end, it would be shortened slightly to Bartel-Pritchard Sq. Service on Prospect Park W between 15 St and 20 St would be provided by the proposed B81. In Red Hook, northbound service would be rerouted to travel directly from Dwight St to Van Dyke St, removing turns and speeding up travel to Downtown Brooklyn. Direct service to IKEA at Beard St/Otsego St would be provided by the proposed B27, B81, and southbound B61.
### [p256_c0019] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p256_c0020] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
[p256_c0021] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p256_c0022] No frequency changes are being proposed at this time other than a slight decrease Sunday Early Morning. No span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p256_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p256_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p256_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 11 12 11 11 21 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 15 12 12 13 20 SUNDAY 24 hours 20 20 13 15 20
```
[p256_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 260

### [p260_c0001] Limited
### [p260_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p260_c0003] B62
### [p260_c0004] Downtown Brooklyn - Astoria
[p260_c0005] Related Routes: B62, B27, B43, B57, Q63, Q66
### [p260_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p260_c0007] Existing: 7.6 miles Proposed: 8.7 miles
### [p260_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p260_c0009] Existing: 870 feet Proposed: 1,459 feet
### [p260_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p260_c0011] Existing: 2.5 per mile Proposed: 1.6 per mile
### [p260_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p260_c0013] Bus:
[p260_c0014] B25, B26, B27, B38, B39, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44 SBS, B45, B48, B52, B53, B54, B57, B61, B63, B65, B66, B67, B69, Q18, Q19, Q39, Q54, Q59, Q63, Q66, Q67, Q68, Q69, Q104
### [p260_c0015] Subway:
[p260_c0016] 2 3 4 5 7 A C E M F G L N R
### [p260_c0017] LIRR:
[p260_c0018] Hunterspoint Avenue
### [p260_c0019] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p260_c0020] As part of both the Brooklyn and Queens Bus Network Redesigns, the proposed B62 would be extended north from Long Island City to Astoria via 21 St, providing new all-day frequent service between Brooklyn and Queens, responding to customer requests for better interborough service. Although the proposed B62 would no longer directly serve Queens Plaza and Queensborough Plaza, service would still be provided by the Queens Bus Network Redesign's proposed Q63 or Q66. In Brooklyn, the proposed B62 would use Flushing Av immediately adjacent to the Brooklyn Navy Yard, providing better connections from that employment center to the north. Service on Park Av would be provided by the proposed B57, and service to York St in Vinegar Hill would be provided by the proposed B27 and B67. Through Williamsburg, the proposed B62 would travel northbound on Bedford Av and southbound on Driggs Av and Lee Av, removing turns and reducing delays. Existing B62 service on Broadway, Division Av, and Kent Av/Wythe Av would be discontinued. The proposed B62 would travel on McGuinness Blvd between Nassau Av/Driggs Av and Freeman St/Green St to provide faster interborough travel. The B43 would continue to serve Manhattan Av.
[p260_c0021] As a Limited route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than Local routes to improve speed and reliability, but still within walking distance.
[p260_c0022] Frequencies would be increased on weekdays to 10-minutes-or-better and service would operate 24 hours on weekdays and weekends. Overnight, the proposed B62 would serve York St in Vinegar Hill via Navy St and Gold St.
### [p260_c0023] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p260_c0024] • Interborough route • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
### [p260_c0025] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p260_c0026] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p260_c0027]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 8 10 10 10 20 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 20 15 10 13 16 SUNDAY 24 hours 27 20 19 15 25
```
[p260_c0028] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

> [p261_c0006] The map illustrates the B62 bus route in Brooklyn and Astoria. The legend identifies the following elements: Existing Service: Solid red line. Added Service: Solid red line. Discontinued Day Service: Dashed red line. Discontinued Service: Dashed black line. Proposed Bus Network: Solid grey line. Subway: Blue line with circles. Accessible Stations: Grey circle with a dot. Future Accessible Stations: Grey circle with a dot. LIRR: Grey line with a square. Institutional Land Uses: Tan shaded areas. Three red callout boxes provide additional information: Discontinued segments would be served by the proposed B27 and B67. (Points to the northern part of the route near Astoria). Discontinued segments would be served by the proposed Q63 and Q66. (Points to the central part of the route near the Pulaski Bridge). Discontinued segment would be served by the proposed B57. (Points to the southern part of the route near Flushing Av). The map also includes a scale bar (0 to 1 mile) and a north arrow in the bottom right corner. An inset map in the bottom right shows the location of the study area within the New York City metropolitan region.

### [p265_c0013] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p265_c0014] The proposed B63 would maintain its existing routing.
[p265_c0015] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p265_c0016] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.

- [p265_c0018] • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor

## Page 269

### [p269_c0001] Local
### [p269_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p269_c0003] B64
### [p269_c0004] Sunset Park - Coney Island
[p269_c0005] Related Routes: B64, B4, B9, B82
### [p269_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p269_c0007] Existing: 6.7 miles Proposed: 7.1 miles
### [p269_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p269_c0009] Existing: 726 feet Proposed: 1,032 feet
### [p269_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p269_c0011] Existing: 2.1 per mile Proposed: 2.4 per mile
### [p269_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p269_c0013] Bus:
[p269_c0014] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B8, B9, B11, B16, B36, B37, B49, B63, B70, B74, B82, B82 SBS
### [p269_c0015] Subway:
[p269_c0016] D N R F Q
### [p269_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p269_c0018] The proposed B64 would be rerouted on its northern end to serve the Sunset Park Waterfront instead of Shore Rd. The route would travel via 4 Av and 60 St and provide new connections to the Brooklyn Army Terminal and NYU Langone Hospital Brooklyn from southern Brooklyn and from the future accessible 59 St N/R express subway station. The B9 would continue to serve Bay Ridge Av west of 4 Av. The proposed B64 would be rerouted on its southern end to travel via Cropsey Av instead of via Harway Av and Stillwell Av, replacing the B82 which is proposed to be shortened and removing a turn and reducing delays. Service on Stillwell Av would be provided by the proposed B4. Service on Harway Av would be discontinued.
[p269_c0019] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p269_c0020] Frequencies would be increased during some time periods on weekdays, Saturdays, and Sundays. Service span would be slightly increased.
### [p269_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p269_c0022] • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Improved ADA access
### [p269_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p269_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p269_c0025]

## Page 273

### [p273_c0001] Local
### [p273_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p273_c0003] B65
### [p273_c0004] Bergen/Dean Streets
[p273_c0005] Service between Downtown Brooklyn and Crown Heights
[p273_c0006] Related Routes: B65, B15, B45
### [p273_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p273_c0008] Existing: 4.4 miles Proposed: 4.1 miles
### [p273_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p273_c0010] Existing: 742 feet Proposed: 1,013 feet
### [p273_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p273_c0012] Existing: 1.2 per mile Proposed: 1.3 per mile
### [p273_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p273_c0014] Bus:
[p273_c0015] B15, B25, B26, B27, B37, B38, B41, B41 XT, B43, B44, B45, B46, B47, B48, B49, B52, B54, B57, B61, B62, B63, B67, B69
### [p273_c0016] Subway:
[p273_c0017] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q D N R F G
### [p273_c0018] LIRR:
[p273_c0019] Atlantic Terminal
### [p273_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p273_c0021] The proposed B65 would be rerouted to travel on Atlantic Av in both directions between Downtown Brooklyn and Washington Av, using a wider street as compared to Dean St/Bergen St and providing a direct connection to the Atlantic Av-Barclays Center subway and LIRR stations. This would also remove a difficult right turn from Bergen St onto Smith St, reducing delays. Existing B65 service on Dean St and Bergen St west of Washington Av would be discontinued. The proposed B65 would also be slightly shortened on its eastern end, terminating at St Marks Av/ Bergen St and Ralph Av. Service on Ralph Av would be provided by the proposed B15 and the proposed B45. Service on Buffalo Av would be discontinued.
[p273_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p273_c0023] Frequencies would be decreased during Weekday Late Evenings and on Saturdays and Sundays but would still match ridership demand. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p273_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p273_c0025] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p273_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p273_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p273_c0028]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 4:30 AM - 1:00 AM 14 15 14 15 23 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 4:30 AM - 1:00 AM 15 15 15 15 19 SUNDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 15 15 15 15 22
```

## Page 277

### [p277_c0001] Local
### [p277_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p277_c0003] B66
### [p277_c0004] Wilson Avenue
[p277_c0005] Service between Williamsburg and Broadway Junction
[p277_c0006] Related Routes: B60
### [p277_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p277_c0008] Existing: N/A
[p277_c0009] Proposed: 4.9 miles
### [p277_c0010] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p277_c0011] Existing: N/A
[p277_c0012] Proposed: 1,145 feet
### [p277_c0013] URNS PER MILE
[p277_c0014] Existing: N/A
[p277_c0015] Proposed: 1.9 per mile
### [p277_c0016] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p277_c0017] Bus:
[p277_c0018] B7, B10, B12, B15, B20, B25, B26, B38, B39, B43, B44 SBS, B52, B53, B54, B57, B60, B62, B69, B76, B83, Q24, Q54, Q56, Q59, Q68
### [p277_c0019] Subway:
[p277_c0020] A C E J M Z L
### [p277_c0021] LIRR:
[p277_c0022] East New York
### [p277_c0023] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p277_c0024] The proposed B66 would be a new route connecting Williamsburg and Broadway Junction. This route would replace existing B60 service between Williamsburg Bridge Plaza and Fulton St, with an extension to the future accessible Broadway Junction A/C/J/Z/L station. Northbound service between Broadway and Wilson Av would be rerouted from narrow Decatur St onto Cooper St to match southbound service, simplifying the route path. Northbound service between Johnson Av and Meserole St would be rerouted from Graham Av onto Bushwick Av to match southbound service and to provide a direct connection to the Montrose Av L station and to the proposed B15.
[p277_c0025] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p277_c0026] Frequencies would be slightly lower compared to the existing B60 but would still match ridership demand. The route would operate 24 hours on weekdays and weekends.
### [p277_c0027] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p277_c0028] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p277_c0029] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p277_c0030] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p277_c0031]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 15 20 14 17 30 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 AM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 29 23 19 24 29 SUNDAY 24 hours 30 27 23 30 30
```
[p277_c0032] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 280

### [p280_c0001] Local
### [p280_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p280_c0003] B67
### [p280_c0004] Kensington - Vinegar Hill
[p280_c0005] Related Routes: B67, B57, B62
### [p280_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p280_c0007] Existing: 7.0 miles Proposed: 5.7 miles
### [p280_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p280_c0009] Existing: 911 feet Proposed: 950 feet
### [p280_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p280_c0011] Existing: 3.4 per mile Proposed: 1.9 per mile
### [p280_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p280_c0013] Bus:
[p280_c0014] B16, B25, B26, B27, B35, B37, B38, B41, B41 XT, B45, B48, B52, B54, B55, B57, B61, B62, B63, B65, B69, B81
### [p280_c0015] Subway:
[p280_c0016] 2 3 4 5 A C B Q D N R F G
### [p280_c0017] LIRR:
[p280_c0018] Atlantic Terminal
### [p280_c0019] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p280_c0020] The proposed B67's northern terminal would be at Sands St/Navy St in Vinegar Hill at all times the route operates, which would be an extension during weekday evenings and on weekends. Service through the Brooklyn Navy Yard to South Williamsburg would be discontinued. The connection between the Navy Yard, Williamsburg, and points north would instead be provided by the proposed B62 LTD on Flushing Av at increased frequencies.
### [p280_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p280_c0022] • Improved stop spacing • Fewer route patterns • Avoids narrow streets • Priority Corridor
[p280_c0023] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p280_c0024] No frequency or service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p280_c0025] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p280_c0026] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p280_c0027]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-11:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 12 22 11 21 30 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 30 30 30 30 30 SUNDAY 4:30 AM - 1:30 AM 30 30 30 30 30
```
[p280_c0028] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 284

### [p284_c0001] Local
### [p284_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p284_c0003] B68
### [p284_c0004] Coney Island Avenue
[p284_c0005] Service between Windsor Terrace and Manhattan Beach
[p284_c0006] Related Routes: B68, B1, B49
### [p284_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p284_c0008] Existing: 7.6 miles Proposed: 7.1 miles
### [p284_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p284_c0010] Existing: 786 feet Proposed: 955 feet
### [p284_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p284_c0012] Existing: 0.7 per mile Proposed: 0.6 per mile
### [p284_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p284_c0014] Bus:
[p284_c0015] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B8, B9, B11, B16, B31, B35, B36, B44 SBS, B49, B55, B61, B81, B82, B82, B100
### [p284_c0016] Subway:
[p284_c0017] B Q F G
### [p284_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p284_c0019] The proposed B68 would mostly maintain its existing routing but would have a new southern terminal at Kingsborough Community College. Instead of traveling west from Coney Island Av to the Coney Island-Stillwell Av D/F/N/Q station, it would travel east to the college via Brighton Beach Av and Oriental Blvd along with the B1. This would simplify service to the college and would allow for better management of overcrowding, as both bus routes to the college would serve the same Brighton Beach B/Q subway station. The proposed B68 would provide similar bus connections to the B49 north of Brighton Beach Av. Service between Coney Island Av and Stillwell Av via Brighton Beach Av, W 5th St, and Neptune Av would be provided by the proposed B49.
[p284_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p284_c0021] No frequency changes are being proposed at this time. Service spans would be slightly shortened overnight, with new 24-hour service provided on the proposed B49 instead.
### [p284_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p284_c0023] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Priority Corridor
### [p284_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p284_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p284_c0026]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 4:00 AM - 2:30 AM 8 9 8 9 23 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 4:00 AM - 2:30 AM 10 8 8 11 18 SUNDAY 4:00 AM - 2:30 AM 13 10 10 10 19
```
[p284_c0027] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 285

### [p285_c0001] Local
[p285_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p285_c0003] B68
### [p285_c0004] Coney Island Avenue
[p285_c0005] Service between Windsor Terrace and Manhattan Beach
[p285_c0006] Related Routes: B68, B1, B49
> [p285_c0007] The map displays the B68 bus route in Brooklyn, running north-south along Coney Island Avenue. The route is shown with a thick green line for 'Added Service' and a dashed black line for 'Discontinued Service'. The map includes a legend in the top right corner with the following symbols: Existing Service (thick green line) Added Service (thick green line) Discontinued Service (dashed black line) Proposed Bus Network (thin grey line) Subway (blue line with circle) Accessible Stations (black circle with dot) Future Accessible Stations (black circle with cross) LIRR (grey rectangle) Institutional Land Uses (orange rectangle) The map shows various neighborhoods including Windsor Terrace, Kensington, Flatbush, Midwood, Bensonhurst, Bath Beach, Gravesend, Marine Park, and Manhattan Beach. Major streets like Prospect Park Ave, Coney Island Ave, and Brighton Beach Av are labeled. A callout box in the bottom left corner states: "Discontinued segment would be served by the proposed B49." An inset map in the bottom left shows the location of the area within the New York City region. A scale bar at the bottom right indicates distances of 0, 0.5, and 1 Miles, accompanied by a north arrow.

## Page 288

### [p288_c0001] Local
### [p288_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p288_c0003] B69
### [p288_c0004] Greenpoint - Windsor Terrace
[p288_c0005] Related Routes: B69, B48, B62, B67, B81
### [p288_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p288_c0007] Existing: 6.2 miles Proposed: 7.6 miles
### [p288_c0008] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p288_c0009] Existing: 748 feet Proposed: 1,002 feet
### [p288_c0010] TURNS PER MILE
[p288_c0011] Existing: 1.8 per mile Proposed: 1.8 per mile
### [p288_c0012] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p288_c0013] Bus:
[p288_c0014] B25, B26, B38, B41, B41 SBS, B43, B44, B44 SBS, B45, B48, B52, B53, B54, B57, B61, B62, B65, B66, B67, B81, Q54, Q59, Q68
### [p288_c0015] Subway:
[p288_c0016] 2 3 B Q C F G L
### [p288_c0017] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p288_c0018] The proposed B69 would be rerouted on its northern end to Greenpoint via the existing B48 travel path, maintaining its connection to much of the Brooklyn Navy Yard while improving connectivity in northern Brooklyn. Connections would now be available to the future accessible Lorimer St J/M and L stations. Service on the western portion of Flushing Av would be provided by the proposed B48 and B62. Service between Park Slope and the western end of the Brooklyn Navy Yard would still be available via the proposed B67. On its southern end, the proposed B69 would be shortened, terminating at Prospect Park W/20 St. Service on McDonald Av would be provided by the proposed B67 and B81.
[p288_c0019] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p288_c0020] No frequency changes are being proposed at this time. Service spans would be lengthened.
### [p288_c0021] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p288_c0022] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p288_c0023] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p288_c0024] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p288_c0025]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM 11 23 14 23 25 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 AM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM 30 27 30 30 30 SUNDAY 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM 30 27 30 30 30
```
[p288_c0026] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

> [p289_c0006] Legend: Existing Service (thick green line) Added Service (thin green line) Discontinued Service (dashed black line) Proposed Bus Network (thin grey line) Subway (blue line with circle) Accessible Stations (grey circle) Future Accessible Stations (black circle) LIRR (grey square) Institutional Land Uses (orange square) Map Details: Existing Service: A thick green line runs along Lorne St from Nassau Av to the intersection with Flushing Av. Added Service: A thin green line branches off Lorne St, heading north towards Nassau Av. Discontinued Service: A dashed black line follows the route from the intersection with Flushing Av southwards along McDonald Av, ending at the intersection with 19 St. Proposed Bus Network: A thin grey line follows the route from the intersection with Flushing Av southwards along McDonald Av, continuing past 19 St. Subway: A blue line with circles runs along the waterfront and through the city. Accessible Stations: Grey circles are located at various stops along the subway and bus routes. Future Accessible Stations: Black circles are located at several stops, including those on the subway and at the intersection of McDonald Av and 19 St. LIRR: A grey square is located at the intersection of McDonald Av and 19 St. Institutional Land Uses: Orange squares are located in various areas, including Prospect Park and Prospect Lefferts Gardens. Callouts: A green box near the intersection of Lorne St and Flushing Av states: "Discontinued segment would be served by the proposed B48 and B62." A green box near the intersection of McDonald Av and 19 St states: "Discontinued segment would be served by the proposed B67 and B81." Inset Map: A circular inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of the B69 route within the context of the entire city of New York. Scale: A scale bar at the bottom right indicates 0, 0.5, and 1 Miles. Compass: A north arrow is located at the bottom right. Source: The map is credited to Canarsie at the bottom right.

## Page 293

### [p293_c0001] Local
### [p293_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p293_c0003] B70 Sunset Park - Dyker Heights Related Routes: B70, B35
### [p293_c0004] ROUTE LENGTH
[p293_c0005] Existing: 4.9 miles Proposed: 4.7 miles
### [p293_c0006] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p293_c0007] Existing: 726 feet Proposed: 1,043 feet
### [p293_c0008] TURNS PER MILE
[p293_c0009] Existing: 2.4 per mile Proposed: 2.5 per mile
### [p293_c0010] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p293_c0011] Bus:
[p293_c0012] B1, B4, B8, B9, B11, B16, B35, B37, B63, B64, S53, S79 SBS, S93
### [p293_c0013] Subway:
[p293_c0014] D N R
### [p293_c0015] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p293_c0016] The proposed B70 would mostly maintain its existing routing except for a new northern terminal at Industry City. Instead of terminating at 39 St/1 Av, the proposed route would terminate at 3 Av/30 St, providing a new connection to the retail area from southwest Brooklyn and from the future accessible 36 St D/N/R station. Service on 39 St west of 3 Av would continue to be provided by the proposed B35.
[p293_c0017] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p293_c0018] Frequencies would be slightly increased during Weekday Middays. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p293_c0019] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p293_c0020] • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Improved ADA access
### [p293_c0021] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p293_c0022] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p293_c0023]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-11:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 4:30 AM - 2:00 AM 10 16 12 14 28 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 4:30 AM - 2:00 AM 18 15 17 20 28 SUNDAY 4:30 AM - 2:00 AM 25 18 20 30 30
```
[p293_c0024] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

### [p297_c0016] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p297_c0017] The proposed B74 would maintain its existing routing.

### [p298_c0003] B74 Mermaid Avenue Service between Coney Island and Sea Gate Related Routes: B74
### [p298_c0004] Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign:
### [p298_c0005] Draft Plan | 297
> [p298_c0006] The map illustrates the B74 bus route in Brooklyn, specifically the Mermaid Avenue section. The route is shown as a thick green line, indicating added service. It starts at the Coney Island station (marked with a subway icon) and travels west along Mermaid Avenue. It then turns north onto W 33 St, then west onto W 37 St, and finally south onto Bayview Avenue. The map also shows the existing bus network (grey lines) and discontinued service (black dashed lines). Key landmarks include Calvert Vaux Park, Coney Island Boat Basin, Kaiser Park, and the W 8 St - NY Aquarium. A legend in the top right corner defines the symbols: Existing Service (thick green line), Added Service (thin green line), Discontinued Service (black dashed line), Proposed Bus Network (grey line), Subway (blue line with circle), Accessible Stations (black circle with dot), Future Accessible Stations (black circle with cross), LIRR (grey square), and Institutional Land Uses (orange area). A scale bar at the bottom left shows distances up to 0.5 miles, and a north arrow is also present. An inset map in the bottom right corner shows the location of the area within the greater New York City region.

## Page 300

### [p300_c0001] Local
### [p300_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p300_c0003] B76
### [p300_c0004] Canarsie - Broadway Junction
[p300_c0005] Related Routes: B17, B42, B60
### [p300_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p300_c0007] Existing: N/A
[p300_c0008] Proposed: 4.7 miles
### [p300_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p300_c0010] Existing: N/A
[p300_c0011] Proposed: 1,015 feet
### [p300_c0012] TURNS PER MILE
[p300_c0013] Existing: N/A
[p300_c0014] Proposed: 1.2 per mile
### [p300_c0015] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p300_c0016] Bus:
[p300_c0017] B5, B6, B6 LTD, B8, B10, B12, B14, B15, B17, B20, B25, B35, B42, B53, B55, B60, B66, B82, B82 SBS, B83, B103, Q24, Q56
### [p300_c0018] Subway:
[p300_c0019] 3 A C J Z L
### [p300_c0020] LIRR:
[p300_c0021] East New York
### [p300_c0022] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p300_c0023] The proposed B76 would be a new route connecting the Paerdegat section of Canarsie with Broadway Junction, replacing the existing part-time B17 Paerdegat branch. The proposed new B76 would connect E 80 St/Paerdegat 15 St and the Canarsie-Rockaway Pkwy L station in the peak hours, traveling via Avenue L and Rockaway Pkwy, and continuing via Rockaway Av to the future accessible Broadway Junction A/C/J/Z/L station. The route would remove turns between Paerdegat and the Canarsie-Rockaway Pkwy L station, reducing delays. The route would also provide additional service during the peaks to the B42 on Rockaway Pkwy and to the B60 on Rockaway Av.
[p300_c0024] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p300_c0025] Frequencies would be similar to the existing B17 Paerdegat branch. Spans would be reduced as compared to the existing B17 Paerdegat branch.
### [p300_c0026] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p300_c0027] • More direct routing • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Avoids narrow streets • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p300_c0028] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p300_c0029] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p300_c0030]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 6:00 AM - 9:00 AM 3:00 PM - 8:00 PM 10 - 10 12 - Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY N/A - - - - - SUNDAY N/A - - - - -
```
[p300_c0031] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

> [p301_c0004] The map illustrates the B76 bus route in Brooklyn, specifically the Canarsie - Broadway Junction area. The route is highlighted in green, indicating it is a new or added service. It starts at Broadway Junction (marked with a subway station icon) and travels south along Rockaway Avenue, passing through Brownsville and Canarsie neighborhoods. The route then turns east along E 80th Street, ending at the intersection with Avenue L. The map also shows the existing bus network in grey, subway lines in blue, and various landmarks such as Crown Heights, Bushwick, and East New York. A legend in the bottom left corner defines the symbols used: Existing Service (dark green line), Added Service (light green line), Discontinued Service (dashed black line), Proposed Bus Network (grey line), Subway (blue line with circle), Accessible Stations (black circle with dot), Future Accessible Stations (grey circle with dot), LIRR (grey square), and Institutional Land Uses (orange square). A scale bar at the bottom right shows distances up to 1 mile, and a north arrow is also present. An inset map in the bottom right corner shows the location of the study area within the state of New York.

## Page 303

### [p303_c0001] Local
### [p303_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p303_c0003] B81
### [p303_c0004] Midwood - Red Hook
[p303_c0005] Related Routes: B57, B61, B67, B69, B103
### [p303_c0006] ROUTE LENGTH
[p303_c0007] Existing: N/A
[p303_c0008] Proposed: 7.1 miles
### [p303_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p303_c0010] Existing: N/A
[p303_c0011] Proposed: 995 feet
### [p303_c0012] URNS PER MILE
[p303_c0013] Existing: N/A
[p303_c0014] Proposed: 2.2 per mile
### [p303_c0015] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p303_c0016] Bus:
[p303_c0017] B5, B6, B6 LTD, B8, B11, B16, B27, B35, B37, B40, B41, B41 XT, B44, B44 SBS, B49, B55, B61, B63, B67, B68, B69, B103, Q35
### [p303_c0018] Subway:
[p303_c0019] 2 5 F G R Q
### [p303_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p303_c0021] The proposed B81 would be a new route connecting Red Hook and Midwood, providing new direct service from Red Hook and Park Slope to central Brooklyn and providing new connections to the Q and 2/5 subway lines as well as high-frequency bus routes such as the B8, B35, B41, and B44. The proposed B81 would follow the B61 travel path from the Red Hook ferry terminal to Prospect Park W/20 St, replacing the shortened B61 on Prospect Park W between Bartel-Pritchard Sq and 20 St. (In Red Hook, the Red Hook-bound B81 would travel via Columbia St for direct service to IKEA, while the proposed B61 would travel directly from Dwight St to Van Dyke St.) In Windsor Terrace and Kensington, the proposed B81 would follow the B69 travel path on McDonald Av, replacing the shortened B69. East of McDonald Av, the proposed B81 would travel via Cortelyou Rd and Flatbush Av, replacing the shortened B103 LTD travel path east of Coney Island Av and providing new service on Cortelyou Rd west of Coney Island Av.
[p303_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p303_c0023] Frequencies and service spans would be similar to existing B103 LTD trips between Midwood and Downtown Brooklyn.
### [p303_c0024] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p303_c0025] • New connections • Improved stop spacing • Improved ADA access • Priority Corridor
### [p303_c0026] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p303_c0027] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p303_c0028]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 5:30 AM - 1:00 AM 10 14 9 12 28 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 AM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 6:00 AM - 1:00 AM 27 18 15 18 20 SUNDAY 6:30 AM - 1:00 AM 27 20 20 20 20
```
[p303_c0029] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.

## Page 304

### [p304_c0001] Local
[p304_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p304_c0003] B81
### [p304_c0004] Midwood - Red Hook
[p304_c0005] Related Routes: B57, B61, B67, B69, B103
> [p304_c0006] The map illustrates the B81 bus route in Brooklyn, connecting Midwood to Red Hook. The route is highlighted in green, indicating it is a new route. It starts at Red Hook (Van Brunt St, Beard St, Lorraine St) and travels east through Gowanus, Park Slope, and Prospect Park, passing through Prospect Park W and McDonald Av. It then heads south through Kensington, Flatbush, and Flatbush Av, ending at Flatbush Av and Brooklyn College. The map also shows the existing bus network in grey, subway lines in blue, and institutional land uses in tan. A legend in the top right corner defines the symbols: Existing Service (thick green line), Added Service (thin green line), Discontinued Service (thick black line), Proposed Bus Network (thin grey line), Subway (blue line with circle), Accessible Stations (black circle with dot), Future Accessible Stations (black circle with cross), LIRR (grey square), and Institutional Land Uses (tan area). A scale bar at the bottom right shows 0, 0.5, and 1 Miles, and a north arrow is also present. An inset map in the bottom left corner shows the location of the B81 route within the city of New York.
[p304_c0007] _Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign: Draft Plan | 303_

## Page 307

### [p307_c0001] Local
### [p307_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p307_c0003] B82
### [p307_c0004] Kings Highway / Flatlands Avenue
[p307_c0005] Service between Gravesend and Spring Creek Towers
[p307_c0006] Related Routes: B82, B6, B64, B82 SBS
### [p307_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p307_c0008] Existing: 10.5 miles Proposed: 9.4 miles
### [p307_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p307_c0010] Existing: 746 feet Proposed: 1,088 feet
### [p307_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p307_c0012] Existing: 1.2 per mile Proposed: 1.3 per mile
### [p307_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p307_c0014] Bus:
[p307_c0015] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B9, B17, B31, B40, B41, B41 XT, B42, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B60, B64, B68, B76, B82 SBS, B83, B100, B103, Q35
### [p307_c0016] Subway:
[p307_c0017] B Q D N F L
### [p307_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p307_c0019] The proposed B82 would be shortened on its southern end, terminating at Cropsey Av/Bay 37 St as the B82 SBS does. The Gravesend-bound B82 would serve Shore Pkwy between Bay Pkwy and 26 Av, replacing existing B6 service. Service on Cropsey Av between 26 Av and Coney Island would be provided by the proposed B64. In Flatlands, the proposed B82 would travel between Kings Hwy and Flatlands Av via Avenue K instead of via Flatbush Av, sharing the same travel path as the B82 SBS, traveling on the Kings Hwy bus lanes for a greater distance, and avoiding congestion on Flatbush Av. Existing service on Flatlands Av between Flatbush Av and Avenue K would be discontinued.
[p307_c0020] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p307_c0021] Frequencies would be slightly increased during Early Evenings on weekends. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p307_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p307_c0023] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor
### [p307_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p307_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p307_c0026]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 9 11 9 11 13 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 8 10 9 9 13 SUNDAY 24 hours 10 10 10 10 18
```

## Page 312

### [p312_c0001] Crosstown/SBS
### [p312_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p312_c0003] B82
### [p312_c0004] Kings Highway / Flatlands Avenue
[p312_c0005] Service between Gravesend and Spring Creek Towers
[p312_c0006] Related Routes: B82 SBS
### [p312_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p312_c0008] Existing: 8.9 miles Proposed: 8.9 miles
### [p312_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p312_c0010] Existing: 2,136 feet Proposed: 2,362 feet
### [p312_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p312_c0012] Existing: 1.2 per mile Proposed: 1.2 per mile
### [p312_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p312_c0014] Bus:
[p312_c0015] B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B6 LTD, B7, B9, B17, B31, B40, B41, B41 XT, B42, B44, B44 SBS, B46, B46 SBS, B47, B49, B60, B64, B68, B76, B82, B83, B100, B103, Q35
### [p312_c0016] Subway:
[p312_c0017] B Q D N F L
### [p312_c0018] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p312_c0019] The proposed B82 SBS would maintain its existing routing.
[p312_c0020] As a Crosstown/SBS route, stops would only be located at key destinations and transfer points to provide faster and more reliable service across the corridor.
[p312_c0021] Frequencies would be increased on weekdays to 10-minutes-or-better. No service span changes are being proposed at this time.
### [p312_c0022] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p312_c0023] • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor
### [p312_c0024] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p312_c0025] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p312_c0026]


## Page 315

### [p315_c0001] Local
### [p315_c0002] New Route Routing Change Schedule Change
### [p315_c0003] B83
### [p315_c0004] Pennsylvania Avenue
[p315_c0005] Service between Broadway Junction and Gateway Center
[p315_c0006] Related Routes: B83, B5, B6 LTD, B15, B20, B55, B103
### [p315_c0007] ROUTE LENGTH
[p315_c0008] Existing: 5.3 miles Proposed: 4.8 miles
### [p315_c0009] AVERAGE STOP SPACING
[p315_c0010] Existing: 1,015 feet Proposed: 1,369 feet
### [p315_c0011] TURNS PER MILE
[p315_c0012] Existing: 1.9 per mile Proposed: 1.3 per mile
### [p315_c0013] PROPOSED CONNECTIONS
### [p315_c0014] Bus:
[p315_c0015] B53, B55, B60, B66, B76, B82, B82 SBS, B103, Q24, Q51, Q56
### [p315_c0016] Subway:
[p315_c0017] 3 A C J Z L
### [p315_c0018] LIRR:
[p315_c0019] East New York
### [p315_c0020] PROPOSED ROUTE SUMMARY
[p315_c0021] The proposed B83 would be streamlined to stay on Pennsylvania Av, providing quicker trips between Spring Creek Towers and Gateway Center, and subway connections at the Pennsylvania Av 3 and Broadway Junction A/C/J/Z/L stations. This reroute would remove turns and reduce delays. Existing service on Vandalia Av and Van Siclen Av would be discontinued, though similar subway connections would be available via the proposed B6 LTD, B5, and B103 on Cozine Av, along with the proposed B20 on Stanley Av, and the B15 and B55 on New Lots Av.
[p315_c0022] As a Local route, stops would be spaced slightly farther apart than existing to speed up buses and improve reliability.
[p315_c0023] Frequencies would be increased during Middays, PM Peak, and Early Evenings on weekdays, as well as during Saturday mornings and Sunday late evenings.
[p315_c0024] Service would now operate 24 hours on weekdays and weekends, though new proposed overnight trips would only operate between Broadway Junction and Spring Creek Towers.
### [p315_c0025] ROUTE IMPROVEMENTS
- [p315_c0026] • More direct routing • Improved stop spacing • Improved frequency • Priority Corridor
### [p315_c0027] PROPOSED FREQUENCY* AND SPAN
[p315_c0028] ● Increased Service ● Decreased Service ● No Change
[p315_c0029]

```text
Service Hours AM Peak 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Midday 9:00 AM-1:59 PM PM Peak 2:00 PM-5:59 PM Early Evening 6:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM WEEKDAY 24 hours 7 8 8 8 17 Service Hours Early Morning 6:00 AM-8:59 AM Morning 9:00 AM-11:59 PM Midday 12:00 PM-4:59 PM Early Evening 5:00 PM-7:59 PM Late Evening 8:00 PM-11:59 PM SATURDAY 24 hours 15 12 10 11 18 SUNDAY 24 hours 20 15 15 15 18
```
[p315_c0030] * Frequencies indicate how often the bus comes on average in the peak direction, in minutes.
